Summary: Security Issue: Running Polyspace or MATLAB with Polyspace installed for R2013b ¨C R2018a may allow remote code execution
Description: Security updates are available for R2013b¨CR2018a. Running MATLAB with Polyspace installed may allow remote code execution.   If you are using R2018b, no action is required. This release is not affected.   If you are using an older release,?upgrade to R2018b .   If you need to use older releases, download security updates linked in the table below.              Release       Solution                     R2018b       No action required                R2018a       Download R2018a Update 6                 R2017b       Download R2017b Update 9                 R2017a       Download R2017a Update 3                 R2016b       Download R2016b Update 6                 R2016a       Download R2016a Update 7                 R2015b       Download R2015b Update 1                 R2015a SP1       Download R2015a SP1 Update 3                 R2013b¨CR2015a       See the Workaround  section below

On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. Summary: Security Issue: MATLAB apps (.mlapp) authored with?App Designer?and run with MATLAB? Runtime for R2016a ¨C R2017a may allow remote file upload and code execution
Description: Security updates are available for MATLAB Runtime R2016a¨CR2017a. This only applies to MATLAB apps (.mlapp) authored with?App Designer ?and run with MATLAB Runtime. If you are using MATLAB Compiler R2016a ¨C R2017a you can recompile and redistribute your apps to use MATLAB Runtime R2017b or greater. If you will continue to use R2016a ¨C R2017a, downstream app users will need to update their MATLAB Runtime. See the Workaround section below for options to provide the MATLAB Runtime update to app users. The base MATLAB Runtime has not been updated. Future deployed apps will need the MATLAB Runtime updates applied.
Workaround: Update your installed MATLAB Runtime:                      Go to the?MATLAB Runtime Download Web Page?to download and install the appropriate MATLAB Runtime Update.                Inform end-users of your apps about the security updates for MATLAB Runtime.                         Make the MATLAB Runtime Update Installer available to the end-users in either of these ways:                                                  Ask them to download the MATLAB Runtime Update Installer from the?MATLAB Runtime Download Web Page?for the MATLAB version that you are using.                            Share the MATLAB Runtime Update that you downloaded in step 1.                                                     Apply the Update to the installed MATLAB Runtime.
Fixed In: R2016a Update 7, R2016b Update 6, R2017a Update 3, R2017b

Summary: Security Issue:?MATLAB Mobile Connector may allow remote file upload and code execution for R2016a ¨C R2018a
Description: Security updates are available for MATLAB R2016a¨CR2018a. Running MATLAB when the MATLAB Mobile Connector is on may allow remote file upload and code execution.   If you are using R2018b, no action is required. This release is not affected.   If you are using an older release,?upgrade to R2018b .   If you need to use older releases, download security updates linked in the table below.              Release       Solution                     R2018b       No action required                R2018a       Download R2018a Update 6                 R2017b       Download R2017b Update 9                 R2017a       Download R2017a Update 3                 R2016b       Download R2016b Update 6                 R2016a       Download R2016a Update 7           The default condition of the MATLAB Mobile Connector is as follows:   For R2017b¨CR2018a, the MATLAB Mobile Connector is off by default. It is on only if you explicitly enable it.   For R2016a¨CR2017a, the MATLAB Mobile Connector is enabled by default.
Fixed In: R2016a Update 7, R2016b Update 6, R2017a Update 3, R2017b Update 9, R2018a Update 6, R2018b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for multiple Unit Delay blocks
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a?model that meets both?of these conditions: More than one Unit Delay block share the same input signal or?the Unit Delay blocks directly connect to?one another. The Unit Delay blocks are in the same reusable subsystem.?  In the incorrect generated code, an uninitialized or undeclared local variable may be used.
Workaround: There are two potential workarounds:In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, on?the Optimization>Signals and Parameters?pane,?set the Optimize global data access parameter None.In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization>Signals and Parameters pane, select the Pack Boolean data into bitfields parameter.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink model with Variant subsystem?might incorrectly generate preprocessor conditionals
Description: Simulink model with Variant subsystem might generate code without preprocessor conditionals when all?of the following conditions are true:  Variant Subsystem is within a conditionally executed subsystem.  Variant Subsystem has an empty default choice.  Option?Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals ?is selected.
Workaround: Place System Outputs block from the Simulink Library Browser inside the empty choice to workaround the behavior.?

Summary: Code generation may fail when the type of a MATLAB Function block input?is not explicitly set
Description: Generating code for a model containing a MATLAB Function block may fail when the type of an input argument is not explicitly set. For example, this bug could occur when the input to a MATLAB Function block is a?masked parameter with an unspecified data type. A masked parameter dialog input that uses a function such as length(x)  could produce a value with an unspecified type. When code generation fails, you get an error message like the following: CGIR assertion 'ValidCore violated: Failed assertion: StaticMatrixType::isa(aExpr->type()) == StaticMatrixType::isa(aExpr->rhs()->type())' failed in 'b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\util_cg\sanitycheckerimpl.cpp:1609'?
Workaround: To workaround this bug, you can explicitly set the types of these inputs. For example, using int32(length(...)) explicitly sets the type of a parameter input.

Summary: Simulink may not report an expected error in 2015b when direct feedback connections exist for nonvirtual subsystems.
Description: Simulink?should report an expected error 'Direct feedback connections involving non-virtual subsystem <subsystem_name> are not allowed'  when the following conditions are true: There is a Width   block or a Constant   block in an atomic subsystem.  The subsystem has a direct feedback connection. Minimize algebraic loop occurrences  ? option is turned on for this subsystem.   However, in 2015b, this error may not be reported if the following additional conditions are true: ?Block reduction ? is set to on for the model.  ?These blocks (Width   block or Constant   block) can be eliminated by the block reduction ? algorithm.   This issue occurs because the Constant   blocks and Width   blocks in the subsystem are set to have a non-constant sample time, instead of the expected behavior of setting constant sample time for these blocks.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when simulating a model after deleting a Dashboard block that was connected to a signal
Description: MATLAB crashes with an access violation when you simulate a model after deleting a Dashboard block that was connected to a signal in the model. The generated stack trace begins with:? [ ?0] 0x00000000fbba17a0 sl_hmi::WebHMI::getModelHandle at b:\matlab\toolbox\simulink\hmi\sl_hmi\signal_observers\webhmi.cpp:550?  [ ?1] 0x00000000fbb56bcc sl_hmi::HMIObserverMessageHandler::handleUpdate at b:\matlab\toolbox\simulink\hmi\sl_hmi\signal_observers\hmiobservermessagehandler.cpp:85
Workaround: After you delete the Dashboard block, remove the streaming badge on the signal that was connected to the Dashboard block. If you want to stream the signal to the Simulation Data Inspector, you can mark the signal for streaming again.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Mfx_Div* replacements can occur in the generated code when using the AUTOSAR 4.0 CRL Library
Description: The following incorrect Mfx_Div*  replacements can occur when generating code using the AUTOSAR 4.0 CRL library:       In1 Type      In2 Type      Output Type      Incorrect Mfx_Div* replacement      Affected release           uint8      int32      uint16      Mfx_Div_s32u16_u16      R2014b - R2016b           int16      int32      int16      Mfx_Div_s32s16_s16      R2014b - R2016b           uint16      int32      uint16      Mfx_Div_s32u16_u16      R2014b - R2016b           uint8      uint32      uint16      Mfx_Div_u32u16_u16      R2014b           uint8      uint32      uint32      Mfx_Div_u32u16_u32      R2014b           uint16      uint32      uint16      Mfx_Div_u32u16_u16      R2014b           uint16      uint32      uint32      Mfx_Div_u32u16_u32      R2014b

Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016a Update 7, R2016b Update 6, R2017a

Summary: Unused variable compiler warning is possible for a model containing Variant Source or Variant Sink blocks
Description: The generated code may contain unguarded local variable definitions for a model containing Variant Source or Variant Sink blocks. These unguarded local variable definitions are possible for the following blocks: Model blocks S-functions Stateflow Charts or MATLAB Function blocks that?have Function Packaging  of Reusable function  Blocks for?viewing simulation results such as?Scope?and Display blocks  The warning?may state that a variable is unused or that a variable is set?but unused. The exact wording of the warning depends on the C compiler.
Fixed In: R2016a Update 7, R2016b Update 6, R2017a, R2017b

Summary: Uncompilable code for a Stateflow chart?inside?of a?Variant subsystem
Description: The code generator may generate uncompilable code for a model that meets the following conditions:? The model contains a Stateflow?chart. In the Block Parameters dialog box, on the Code?Generation  tab, the Function packaging  parameter is set to Reusable function .? The Stateflow chart is inside of?a Variant subsystem.  In the uncompilable code, the chart's initialization function contains initialization statements for variables that are local to the output function and not the initialization function. The compiler error occurs because the initialization function does not contain the declarations for these variables.? ?
Workaround: For the Stateflow chart, change the function packaging parameter?to Nonreusable function or Inlined.?

Summary: Incorrect conditions guarding local variables result in compiler warnings
Description: Compiler warnings about unused variables are possible for a model that meets the following conditions:? The model contains a Variant Source block.? The?model contains a Root Inport block or?Constant block that drives a Variant Source block as its only input. The ground value is assigned to a local variable representing the output of the Variant Source block for the inactive variant.?  Preprocessor conditionals surround the local variable assignment.?  The unused variable warning occurs because the preprocessor conditionals that guard the local variable are not consistent with the usage of the local variable.? ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library Mfx_MulShRight_* entries can provide incorrect calculation
Description: AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library Mfx_MulShRight_*  entries can provide incorrect calculation for a fixed-point multiplication operation The Mfx_MulShRight_*  entries calculate the net slope adjustment factor incorrectly  as: ?????output_slope_adjustment_factor * input1_slope_adjustment_factor / input2_slope_adjustment_factor   The correct net slope adjustment factor calculation is:  ?????input1_slope_adjustment_factor * input2_slope_adjustment_factor / output_slope_adjustment_factor  When the correct/ true  net slope adjustment factor is NOT equal to 1 but the incorrect  net slope adjustment factor is equal to 1 so that a Mfx_MulShRight_*  replacement occurs, the code replacement in the generated code can produce incorrect numerical results.
Workaround: Visit Bug Report ID 1435708 to download a patch that addresses this and other bugs.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code may occur when virtual blocks reorder the input data to reduced block
Description: Incorrect generated code may occur for a model that meets the following conditions:?   The optimization parameters Block Reduction  and Signal Storage?Reuse  are set to on .? Block reduction  removes a block in an active code path. Blocks?that are on?dead code paths are not susceptible to this bug. The input data to the reduced block is not in the same order as the data source. This could happen for a model that contains a Mux block or a Bus Creator block that outputs a virtual bus. The input to the Mux block or Bus Creator block are elements of a single buffer. These elements could be the?result of a Demux block or a Bus Selector block. The blocks participating in the above code path are part of the same atomic subsystem.  In the incorrect generated code, the buffer of the block driving the reduced block?may be?incorrectly reused?such that?another block overwrites the value before all uses of the original block execute. The?workaround section contains an attached example?model that?demonstrates this bug. ?In the example model, the demux/mux structure reorders elements of the Sum block before they are input to the Gain block.??In the generated code, the Gain block is reduced because it has a value of 1 . ??The Subtract block?requires the results of the Sum and Product?blocks. The Product block reuses the?Sum block?buffer. As a result, the Subtract block incorrectly uses the second element of the result of the?Product block instead of the second element of the result of the Sum block. The code snippet below shows this incorrect code (generated using R2014a without the workaround): ? ?for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {  ? ? g1475400_Y.Out1[i] = g1475400_U.In1[i] + g1475400_ConstP.Constant_Value[i]; // Sum Block output   ? ? g1475400_Y.Out3[i] = g1475400_Y.Out1[i] + -4.0;  ? ? g1475400_Y.Out1[i] *= g1475400_U.In2[i]; // Overwritten by Product Block output   ? ? g1475400_Y.Out2[i] = g1475400_Y.Out1[i] + -3.0;  ? }    ? tmp = g1475400_Y.Out1[1]; // Ends up getting result of Product Block rather than Sum Block  ? for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {  ? ? g1475400_Y.Out1[i] = (tmp - g1475400_Y.Out1[i]) + -2.0;  ? }      Examples of blocks that can be removed by block reduction - these will all be cases where the actual data is unchanged by the block calculation:  Gain blocks with a non-tunable value of 1   Concatenate blocks with a single input Absolute value blocks with an unsigned  input datatype Reshape blocks Shift block with shift amount of 0  Permute block with dimensions specified for no permutation Single input Logical AND / OR block Bias block with bias amount of 0  Switch block with non-tunable constant threshold and control port is constant? Delay block with delay 0
Workaround: To avoid this bug, you can use the attached patch to disable some optimizations?pertaining to?virtual bus selection. ?To do so, insert the p-file in the MATLAB path and each time you start MATLAB, enter the following in the MATLAB Command Window:run?workaround_g1475400.pDisabling these?optimizations?may result in more memory usage and data copies for virtual buses, but the code efficiency for demux/mux operations?does not change.Alternatively, you can?set the Block Reduction parameter to off. Changing this parameter?setting will likely result in much more memory usage and data copies than the using the attached patch.
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016a Update 7, R2016b Update 6, R2017a

Summary: AUTOSAR model with root outport of enumerated type fed by nonreusable subsystem generates uncompilable C code
Description: An AUTOSAR model generates uncompilable C code if both of the following conditions exist:  The model contains a root output for which the data type is an enumerated type. The source for the root outport is a subsystem for which?nonreusable function packaging is configured.   Under these conditions, when you generate code, the Rte_Write  call for the outport appears in both the main runnable function and the nonreusable function. The Rte_Write  call for the main runnable function has an uninitialized temporary local variable as an input, which causes a C compilation error. ??
Workaround: To work around this bug, for each root outport of enumerated type fed by a nonreusable subsystem, cast the root outport to an integer type. The runnable reading the signal then casts from integer to enumerated type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch between simulation and code generation results because of uninitialized local variables for Unit Delay blocks
Description: The generated code may contain uninitialized local variables leading to a mismatch between simulation and code generation results for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains one or multiple Switch blocks. The output of one Switch block connects to a Unit Delay block. The Unit Delay block connects to another Switch block if there are multiple Switch blocks or the input port of the only Switch block if there is just one Switch block. If there are multiple Switch blocks, the second Switch block passes the input from the Unit Delay block when the value of its second input evaluates to true.
Workaround: Unzip the attached P-file, workaroundForBugReport1437314.p. Insert the P-file on the MATLAB file path.For every new MATLAB session, in the Command Window, enter the following command: run workaroundForBugReport1437314.p?

Summary: Inconsistent model checksums in the?System Design Description reports
Description: The model checksums in the?locally?generated System Design Description (SDD) reports can differ from the checksums provided in the reference SDD reports (pre-generated?version). The locally generated version of the model checksums depend on the execution environment and can be environment-specific. The execution environment includes aspects such as the MATLAB installation, compiler configuration, and platform (win32/64/Linux). ?If the execution environment of the locally generated version is not the same as the execution environment used to generate the reference reports (pre-generated version), the model checksums can differ.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for Stateflow Chart may be incorrect when a variable is used in multiple parallel states
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a Stateflow Chart that meets these conditions: The Stateflow Chart contains multiple parallel states. At least two of the parallel states use the same variable. For the states that use the variable, the Function Inline Option  parameter is set to Auto  or Function . The variable is used in an entry action, during action, and transition.  The code generator may incorrectly localize the variable and eliminate its assignment statement.
Workaround: You can workaround this issue by implementing one of the following options:1. For the parallel states, set the Function Inline Option parameter to Inline.or2. Run the attached p script to disable the enhanced localization optimization. Note, by disabling this optimization, other variables in your model may not be localized resulting in increased RAM and ROM consumption.

Summary: MATLAB can crash when saving a figure with an invisible colorbar
Description: MATLAB can crash if you save a figure with a colorbar whose Visible property is set to off .

Summary: Possible run-time crash when executing code compiled with Microsoft? Visual Studio? 2013 on hardware without AVX2 instructions
Description: If you compile C++ code generated for a 64-bit application on hardware that does not support AVX2 instructions, the application can crash. The crash is due to a  Microsoft Visual Studio 2013 bug which generates AVX2 instructions for hardware that does not support them. The application terminates with an  error like:    0xC000001D: Illegal Instruction For more information, see this Microsoft article .
Workaround: Use a compiler other than Microsoft Visual Studio 2013 or try the solutions proposed in this Microsoft article.

Summary: Generated code for export-function model containing inline variants may contain extra temporary variables
Description: The generated code may contain extra temporary variables for a model that meets the following conditions: The model is set-up to export functions. The Function-call block contains a Variant Source block or a Variant Sink block. The inputs to the Function-call block have different variant conditions than the Function-call block.  Extra temporary variables and their associated data copies can reduce the performance of the generated code.
Workaround: To avoid this bug, try to set-up your model so that the inputs to the Function-call block have the same variant condition as Function-call block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant block feeding reusable subsystem may lead to incorrect code generation
Description: For a model that meets the following conditions, the code generator might generate incorrect code for the reusable subsystem output function: The model contains at least two instances of the same reusable subsystem. Each reusable subsystem has a Constant block as an input. Each Constant block has a different Constant value  parameter. For the Sample time  parameter, the Constant blocks have the default value of inf . In the Model Configuration parameters dialog box, on the Optimization>Signals and Parameters  pane, the Default parameter behavior  parameter is set to Inlined  and the Inline invariant signals  parameter is selected.

Summary: Viewing certain bus objects in the BusEditor can crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB can crash when you view in the Bus Editor bus objects that have a bus element whose DataType  field is specified using fixdt expressions with the DataTypeOverride  field option.  X = Simulink.Bus; X.Elements(1) = Simulink.BusElement; X.Elements(1).DataType = 'fixdt(''int16'',''DataTypeOverride'',''Off'')'; buseditor  MATLAB can crash when clicking on X  in the Bus Editor.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code may be incorrect for a model that contains chained Merge blocks that span reusable subsystem boundaries
Description: When Merge blocks cross reusable subsystem1  boundaries, the generated code may fail to pass the output signal to the function call resulting in incorrect code. The code generator may generate this code for a model that meets the following conditions. Note that the subsystem names in parentheses refer to the names in the example model attached to this EBR. The model contains a reusable subsystem (ss_bottom ) that contains multiple blocks that connect to a Mux block. The outport of the Mux block connects to the outport of ss_bottom . A reusable subsystem (ss_middle ) contains ss_bottom  and a Merge block. The outport of the Merge block connects to the outport of ss_middle . The above Merge block connects to another Merge block in a parent subsystem. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization>Signals and Parameters  pane, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Individual arguments .  The attached example model and images and the code snippet below show a case in which the generated code is uncompilable because g1388596_example_B  is only accessible in the local scope. For some models, g1388596_example_B  may be defined globally. For those models, the generated code compiles but could possiby give wrong answers. void g1388596_example_SS_middle(real32_T rtu_AThreshStat) {  if (rtu_AThreshStat == 1.0F) {g1388596_example_SS_bottom(&g1388596_example_B->Merge[0],&g1388596_example_B->Merge[1]);   }  }void g1388596_example_step(RT_MODEL_g1388596_example *const g1388596_example_M,  real32_T g1388596_example_U_DIN1, real32_T g1388596_example_U_DIN3, real_T  g1388596_example_Y_RootOutPort[2]){...  if (!(g1388596_example_U_DIN1 == 1.0F)) {g1388596_example_SS_middle(g1388596_example_U_DIN3);   }...  }  The generated code does not contain the output of SS_middle  in its function call. The definition of SS_middle  instead refers to a variable (g1388596_example_B->Merge ) that is only accessible inside the top level model. The following code snippet shows the correct generated code: void g1388596_example_SS_middle(real32_T rtu_AThreshStat, real_T rty_MidOutPort[2])  {  if (rtu_AThreshStat == 1.0F) { g1388596_example_SS_bottom(&rty_MidOutPort[0], &rty_MidOutPort[1]);   }  }  void g1388596_example_step(RT_MODEL_g1388596_example *const g1388596_example_M,  real32_T g1388596_example_U_DIN1, real32_T g1388596_example_U_DIN3, real_T  g1388596_example_Y_RootOutPort[2]){  ...  if (!(g1388596_example_U_DIN1 == 1.0F)) {g1388596_example_SS_middle(g1388596_example_U_DIN3,  g1388596_example_B->Merge);   }  ...}  The correct code contains the output of ss_middle  (that is, rty_MidOutPort ) in its function call. 1  For a subsystem, the code format may be reusable if on the Block Parameters dialog box, the Code Generation>Function packaging  parameter is set to Reusable function  or Auto .
Workaround: The following are some possible workarounds:For multiple Merge blocks in a chain, only the last one in the chain should connect to a reusable subsystem outport.Insert a nonvirtual block in between the Merge blocks. Note that this workaround causes extra memory consumption since previously all the inputs to the Merge block share the same variable.Do not connect multiple blocks to a Mux block and then connect this Mux block directly to a chain of Merge blocks.Change subsystem packaging to inline or nonreusable.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when building a Simulink model that contains Variant blocks with nested Simulink.Variant objects
Description: Building a Simulink model might crash MATLAB if the following conditions are true:?  The models contains Variant blocks such as?Variant Subsystems, Variant Sources, Variant Sinks or Model Variants The condition of a Simulink.Variant  object includes other Simulink.Variant  objects  Consider a model that contains a Variant Subsystem block with two variant choices, Variant1  and Variant2 .? Variant1  is a Simulink.Variant  object with condition 'Variant1a && Variant1b' : Variant1 = Simulink.Variant('Variant1a && Variant1b');?   Variant1a  and Variant1b  are also Simulink.Variant  objects defined as follows: Variant1a?= Simulink.Variant('Var1 == 1');?  Variant1b?= Simulink.Variant('Var2 == 1');  Var1  and Var2  are MATLAB variables. This makes?Variant1  a nested Simulink.Variant  object.
Workaround: Avoid using Simulink.Variant objects within the Condition property of Simulink.Variant objects.In the example in the description above, the condition property of Variant1 can be redefined as:Variant1 = Simulink.Variant('Var1 == 1 && Var2 == 1'); ?

Summary: Simulink models with a particular configuration of conditionally executed subsystems, virtual signal-routing blocks and switches may produce incorrect results in normal-mode simulations.
Description: A Simulink model may produce incorrect results in normal-mode simulations if: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem,   Within that subsystem is a non-virtual block outputting a non-scalar signal,   The non-scalar signal is split by a virtual signal-routing block (such as a selector or demux block) immediately before being output from the conditionally executed subsystem,   Immediately after being output from the subsystem, the non-scalar signal is immediately recombined by a mux block and fed into a switch block,   The model's "Underspecified initialization detection" diagnostic is set to "Simplified"
Workaround: This problem can be fixed by placing a signal-conversion block in-between the mux block and the switch block mentioned in the bug description.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for an integer parameter with a custom storage class of Define or ImportedDefine may cause arithmetic overflow
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a Simulink.Parameter  that meets the following conditions:  The parameter has a Fixed point  or Built in  integer data type. The parameter has a Custom Storage Class of Define  or ImportedDefine . The parameter specifies a symbolic dimension and is used elsewhere in the model as part of an arithmetic expression.  In the incorrect generated code, the C compiler may treat the value of the parameter?as a different data type than is?in the Simulink model. Such a data type mismatch may cause arithmetic operations to yield incorrect results. For example, a parameter of type unsigned short  in a model may be an unsigned int  in the generated C code when it should be cast to a signed int . As a result, signed int  values may be incorrectly treated as unsigned values. When a negative int  value is wrongly interpreted as a large unsigned int  value, integer overflow occurs.
Workaround: If you have your own TLC file for the ImportedDefine or Define custom storage class, generate a data type cast to explicitly convert integers of mismatched type to the correct integer data type used in model. Perform the cast either in a?#define?statement or in the expression where the variable is referenced.

Summary: The singular value decomposition (SVD) and orthogonal-triangular  decomposition (QR) return inaccurate answers for 130-by-65 and 132-by-66 matrices.
Description: When computing the SVD or QR decomposition of a matrix, the computed results typically have a small norm-wise residual. For example, >> rng('default'); >> A = rand(100,50); >> [U,S,V] = svd(A); >> ResSVD = norm(U*S*V'-A)./norm(A)     ResSVD =   ???2.4081e-15   >> [Q,R] = qr(A); >> ResQR = norm(Q*R-A)/norm(A)   ResQR =   ???2.9105e-16   However, the accuracy of these calculations suffers for matrices that are exactly 130-by-65 or 132-by-66 in size. >> rng('default'); >> A = rand(130,65); >> [U,S,V] = svd(A); >> ResSVD = norm(U*S*V'-A)./norm(A)   ResSVD =   ???0.0070   >> [Q,R] = qr(A); >> ResQR = norm(Q*R-A)/norm(A)   ResQR =   ???0.0057
Workaround: You can avoid this problem by padding the matrix with a column of zeros.>> rng('default'); >> A = rand(130,65); >> A = [A,zeros(size(A,1),1)]; >> [U,S,V] = svd(A); >> ResSVD = norm(U*S*V'-A)./norm(A)  ResSVD =  ???1.5074e-15

Summary: Uncompilable code may be generated for custom S-function that has expression folding enabled
Description: The code generator may generate uncompilable code for a top-level model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a function-call subsystem. The function-call subsystem contains a custom S-function block which uses the function LibBlockSetIsExpressionCompliant()  to enable expression folding.  The generated code may be uncompilable due to uninitialized variable(s). ?
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:Extract the files from the .zip file.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Code replacement example for lookup table function results in build error
Description: When you build the example model lookup1d.slx  to test the code replacement table entry you create by following procedures in documentation topic Lookup Table Function Code Replacement  , the following build error occurs: ### Build procedure for model: 'lookup1d' aborted due to an error.  Error: Error(s) encountered while building "lookup1d":  ### Failed to generate all binary outputs.  ? ?
Workaround: Add the following line of code above the function prototype in the example header file myLookup1D.h:#include "rtwtypes.h"

Summary: Failed assertion on second call to cvsim for model with function-call model reference
Description: For a model which contains a function-call model reference, an assertion failure occurs when the following actions are taken: The top model is simulated for coverage using the cvsim  command. After the simulation completes, the referenced model is closed. (The referenced model may or may not be reopened at this point. The issue occurs in either case.) The top model is again simulated for coverage using the cvsim  command.  After executing these steps, a message similar to the following will be shown: failed assertion in b:\matlab\toolbox\slvnv\simcoverage\src\slvnv\utils\object.cpp at line 2843: object[id]!=NULL After this assertion, the cvsim  command may still return a cvdata  object that can be used to generate a coverage report. However, this coverage data is not valid.
Workaround: Ensure that the function-call referenced models are not closed in between subsequent simulations for coverage. If a referenced model is closed after simulating for coverage, close and reopen the top model before simulating again.

Summary: Renaming signal using command line does not modify Bus Selector block output signal name
Description: If a signal X is combined into a bus using Bus Creator, then this signal is selected out using a Bus Selector. If you then change the signal name from its source block output port, the new signal name can be propagated to the output of the Bus Selector block. However, changing signal name from the signal source using the below command line does not modify Bus Selector block output signal name. hp=get_param(gcb, 'PortHandles');set_param(hp.Outport,'Name','Y');
Workaround: Use this command to set the outport name:set_param(hp.Outport,'SignalNameFromLabel','Y');

Summary: AUTOSAR model build fails when imported enumeration is redefined using Simulink enumeration option AddClassNameToEnumNames
Description: Building an AUTOSAR models fails if both of the following conditions exist: You create a Simulink model by importing an AUTOSAR software component from arxml  files. The arxml  code contains enumerated literals and the importer creates corresponding Simulink enumerated types. You modify a generated Simulink enumerated type by resetting option AddClassNameToEnumNames  from false  to true .  Under these conditions, building the model might fail with an assertion. The error message states that an enumeration literal object is not present.
Workaround: To work around this bug, modify the source arxml code to prefix the class name to enumerated literals. This modification causes the arxml importer to create Simulink enumerated types with AddClassNameToEnumName already set to true.Suppose that you have arxml code with enumerated literals Disarm and Arm. For example:<COMPU-METHOD>  <SHORT-NAME>ArmingType</SHORT-NAME>  <CATEGORY>TEXTTABLE</CATEGORY>  <COMPU-SCALE>    <LOWER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">0</LOWER-LIMIT>    <UPPER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">0</UPPER-LIMIT>    <COMPU-CONST>      <VT>Disarm</VT>    </COMPU-CONST>  </COMPU-SCALE>  <COMPU-SCALE>    <LOWER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">1</LOWER-LIMIT>    <UPPER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">1</UPPER-LIMIT>    <COMPU-CONST>      <VT>Arm</VT>    </COMPU-CONST>  </COMPU-SCALE></COMPU-METHOD>Import of the arxml code creates the following enumeration type:Simulink.defineIntEnumType( 'ArmingType', ... {'Disarm','Arm'}, ... [0 1], ... 'DefaultValue', 'Disarm', ... 'Description', 'Type definition of ArmingType.', ... 'HeaderFile', 'Rte_Type.h', ... 'AddClassNameToEnumNames', false);Modify the arxml definitions of the enumeration literals to add the prefix ArmingType_.<COMPU-METHOD>  <SHORT-NAME>ArmingType</SHORT-NAME>  <CATEGORY>TEXTTABLE</CATEGORY>  <COMPU-SCALE>    <LOWER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">0</LOWER-LIMIT>    <UPPER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">0</UPPER-LIMIT>    <COMPU-CONST>      <VT>ArmingType_Disarm</VT>    </COMPU-CONST>  </COMPU-SCALE>  <COMPU-SCALE>    <LOWER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">1</LOWER-LIMIT>    <UPPER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">1</UPPER-LIMIT>    <COMPU-CONST>      <VT>ArmingType_Arm</VT>    </COMPU-CONST>  </COMPU-SCALE></COMPU-METHOD>Import of the modified arxml code creates the enumeration type with AddClassNameToEnumNames set to true.Simulink.defineIntEnumType( 'ArmingType', ... {'ArmingType_Disarm','ArmingType_Arm'}, ... [0 1], ... 'DefaultValue', 'ArmingType_Disarm', ... 'Description', 'Type definition of ArmingType.', ... 'HeaderFile', 'Rte_Type.h', ... 'AddClassNameToEnumNames', true);

Summary: Incorrect code generation for a model containing a Bus Creator block that outputs a nonvirtual bus
Description: Embedded Coder may generate incorrect code for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Bus Creator block. On the Bus Creator Block Parameters dialog box, the Output as nonvirtual bus  parameter is set to on . On the All Parameters  tab, the Optimize global data access  parameter is set to Use global to hold temporary results .  ?

Summary: Incorrect model arguments passed to the mask parameters inside multi-instance normal mode model references
Description: For a multi-instance referenced model in normal mode, the model argument values passed to the mask parameters within the referenced model are incorrect. For a referenced model that has Diagram > Subsystem & Model Reference > Model Block Normal Mode  Visibility  selected or is in accelerator mode, the model argument values that are passed to the mask parameter are correct.
Workaround: To resolve this issue, use one of these options:? Run the top model using accelerator mode.? Set the simulation mode to accelerator if the referenced model has  Model Block Normal Mode Visibility cleared.

Summary: Composing rounding functions (floor, ceil, fix, and round) directly can produce wrong answers
Description: If you compose rounding functions directly, for example in floor(3.5*floor(x)) , you get a wrong answer in the form of a delay.
Workaround: Create an intermediate variable. For example, if the original equation is: y == floor(3.5*floor(x)); then add a new variable, z, and replace the equation with the following two: z == floor(x); y == floor(3.5*z);

Summary: Server starts a worker using an incorrect MATLAB Runtime version
Description: If you have configured a MATLAB Production Server (MPS) instance to work with two different MATLAB Runtime versions, one from R2015b and another R2016a, you will get the following generic error message: "Component could not be initialized."?  This error message is seen by an MPS client in the form of an HTTP 500 server error. The exact scenario when you will encounter this error is as follows:  MPS is configured to work with MATLAB Runtime (R2015b) and MATLAB Runtime (R2016a), and MATLAB Runtime (2015b) is specified as the first entry in the serverˇŻs main_config  file, and The deployed archive being called is created using MATLAB (R2016a).   ? ? ? ? OR  The deployed archive being called is created using MATLAB (R2016a), and MATLAB Runtime (2015b) is the only available MATLAB Runtime version.?   MPS is expected to produce a specific error message such as, "CTF not supported by the installed MATLAB Runtime versions" , but generates the aforementioned generic message.  This behavior occurs due to a bug in MATLAB Runtime (R2015b) where it cannot properly distinguish between archives created using different versions of MATLAB.
Workaround: To address this problem, specify MATLAB Runtime (R2016a) is specified as the first entry in the serverˇŻs main_config file. For example:--mcr-root C:\Program Files\MATLAB\MATLAB Runtime\v901?--mcr-root C:\Program Files\MATLAB\MATLAB Runtime\v90?

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly report dead logic for blocks which are driven by certain types of blocks such as Mean block
Description: Simulink blocks such as Mean block compute their output by iterating over the input data in a loop. If a model consists of such blocks with computational loop then Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly report dead logic for blocks which are driven by these blocks.

Summary: HDL Code generation may error out when the design has a Delay block with enable or reset.
Description: When your Simulink design has a Delay block with an enable port or a reset port, and when the following conditions are all satisfied: Delay length of the block is greater than 1, and the initial value is a non-zero value. Design has multiple rates or optimizations are turned on for the subsystem containing the Delay block.  HDL Code generation may fail with following error: lowering pir native comps to MATLAB code failed with the error message: MATLAB:getReshapeDims:notSameNumel: To RESHAPE the number of elements must not change. Additionally, the generated model may fail to update (Ctrl+D) with following error: Error in port widths or dimensions. < block that has issue >. This port does not accept the dimensions (or orientation) specified by the output signal. Also, in cases where the delay length is very large ( > 200), model generation may take long time to complete.
Workaround: There are no known workarounds.

Summary: On Mac platform, areas in models can cause Simulink to crash
Description: On Mac, models containing multiple Simulink areas can randomly crash. Moving any block or annotation in a subsystem that contains areas can trigger the crash.

Summary: Inserting a block using Quick Block Insert crashes MATLAB
Description: MATLAB crashes while inserting a block using the Quick Block Insert feature when both  of these conditions are met: - You type the name of the block on the Simulink editor canvas.- There is a slblocks.m  file in MATLAB path that is not a valid MATLAB function file. The crash displays the following stack trace: [ 0] 0x00000000fe80462a fl::diag::windows::context_base::capture_data[ 1] 0x00000000fe800ee4 `anonymous namespace'::terminate_impl::log<char const * __ptr64>[ 2] 0x00000000fe8008db `anonymous namespace'::terminate_impl::execute<char const * __ptr64>[ 3] 0x00000000fe8040be fl::diag::terminate[ 4] 0x0000000039e45eda GLEE::assertNoThrow[ 5] 0x0000000039f2dca1 `GLIM::ConnectedPacket::deliver'::`1'::catch$0[ 6] 0x000007feddfa6280 _unDNameEx+00000632[ 7] 0x000007feddf9ddb9 _BuildCatchObjectHelper+00000757[ 8] 0x000000007765b7e1 RtlRestoreContext+00000738[ 9] 0x0000000039eb179d GLIM::ConnectedPacket::deliver[ 10] 0x0000000039eb6037 GLIM::Thread::processCurrentQueue Here is an example of an incorrect slblocks.m  file. Notice that here slblocks.m  is a MATLAB script and not a valid MATLAB function file. % function aBlkStruct = slblocks Browser.Library = 'testlib'; Browser.Name = 'Test Geck Library'; aBlkStruct.Browser = Browser;
Workaround: To correct this issues convert the slblocks.m file to a valid MATLAB function file. For more information, see Add Libraries to the Library Browser.

Summary: DOM-generated hyperlinks with spaces in  target name fail in Microsoft Word documents
Description: Using the DOM API to create a hyperlink to a target in the same document fails in Microsoft Word output if the name of the target contains spaces. For example, this script, which uses the target name 'A B' , creates a broken link in Word output. import mlreportgen.dom.* d = Document('test','docx'); append(d,InternalLink('A B','Text to link from')); p = Paragraph(LinkTarget('A B')); append(p,'Target to link to'); p.Style = {PageBreakBefore}; append(d,p); close(d); rptview(d.OutputPath);
Workaround: For Word output, avoid using spaces in the target name while programmatically creating hyperlinks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model blocks with multiple function-call input ports driven by Function Call Split blocks using Simulink Accelerator execute the wrong function calls during simulation
Description: Model blocks with multiple function-call input ports that are driven by Function Call Split blocks when used in a top model simulated using Simulink Accelerator either fails to generate code or executes the wrong function call port during simulation, leading to incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: Change the simulation mode of the top model to something other than Accelerator.

If you cast a boolean expression to that effectively boolean type, rule 10.3 is not violated because the underlying type is the same. ?However, Polyspace sometimes flags a violation of rule 10.3.

Summary: Unused global variable created in the presence of constant sample time inputs
Description: The generated code may contain defined but unused global variables if the following conditions are true for a model: The model contains one or more source blocks for which the Sample time  parameter is set to [inf,0]  or [inf] . The generated code for these source blocks is in the model_initialize  function.

Summary: MATLAB can crash when dragging blocks from a library into Simulink Editor
Description: When adding?some blocks to a model by dragging it from the Library Browser to the Simulink Editor, MATLAB can crash. The crash can occur with masked subsystems and might occur with other library blocks.? Adding blocks using?the quick insert menu on the model canvas or add_block  does not cause a crash.

Summary: Alt+1 does not work for Zoom 100% in Mac OSX
Description: Simulink documentation for R2015b lists the shortcut for Zoom 100% as Alt+1 . However, this shortcut does not work on Mac OSX.
Workaround: To do so, edit the file toolbox/simulink/simulink/+SLStudio/ViewMenu.xml.1. Find the code that looks like this:   <action type="dynamic" id="Simulink:ZoomNormalView">     <generator gateway="SLStudio.ViewMenu" function="ZoomNormalView" />     <accels>Alt+1</accels>   </action>2. Summary: truncate method does not work for kernel smooth probability distributions
Description: The fitdist  function can fit a variety of distributions to data, including a kernel-smooth density estimate:     p = fitdist(x,'kernel') The resulting distribution object has a truncate  method that is intended to limit the distribution to a lower and upper bound. However, the truncate  method fails for a 'kernel' distribution, with this error message:  Undefined function 'invalidateFit' for input arguments of type 'prob.KernelDistribution'.
Workaround: When using this distribution to generate random numbers, consider generating values from the full (untruncated) distribution and discarding the ones that do not fall between the desired bounds.

Summary: Exported lines and markers are incorrect when output size exceeds screen limits
Description: Printing or saving a MATLAB figure with an output size greater than the screen size can cause line widths and marker sizes to scale unexpectedly. This results in proportionally larger lines and markers in the generated output file. In the files generated below you will notice that the lines and markers appear larger in the "toobig.png" file than they do in either of the others. % create a figure and plot some datafigure;axes('ylim', [0 1], 'xlim', [0 1]);l = line([.2 .8], [.3 .3], 'LineWidth', 9, 'Marker', 'o', 'MarkerSize', 18);  % determine screen resolutionres = get(0, 'ScreenPixelsPerInch'); % determine how big the screen is, in inches scrSize = get(0, 'ScreenSize') / res;  % generate output that matches the screen size outputSize = [0 0 scrSize(3:4)];set(gcf,'PaperUnits', 'inches', 'PaperPosition', outputSize);print('-dpng', 'bscreensize.png', ['-r' num2str(res)]);  % generate output that is smaller than the screen size smallerSize = [0 0 scrSize(3:4) / 2];set(gcf,'PaperUnits', 'inches', 'PaperPosition', outputSize);print('-dpng', 'bsmaller.png', ['-r' num2str(res)]);  % generate output that is sized larger than the screen size bigSize = [0 0 scrSize(3:4) * 3];set(gcf,'PaperUnits', 'inches', 'PaperPosition', bigSize);print('-dpng', 'btoobig.png', ['-r' num2str(res)]);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible rounding error or overflow due to incorrect translation of division and multiplication operations
Description: If the design of a model results in division or multiplication operations involving fixed-point or integer types, incorrect overflow or rounding error might occur when: In the case of division, the denominator is reduced to a constant value of?-1 In the case of multiplication, either operand?is reduced to a constant value of -1  The incorrect overflow only occurs if the other operand is a signed type and it takes on the minimum negative value for its type during runtime.  For the rounding error to occur, the type of the other operand must be fixed-point and have a fractional part.

Summary: Error when generating code for some Simulink Functions that return a single boolean output
Description: When you generate code for a Stateflow chart that calls an external Simulink Function, MATLAB throws an assertion error when both of the following conditions are true: The external Simulink Function has a single boolean output The function is used as the only expression in an if  statement or a transition condition in the chart  Note that neither calling Simulink Functions defined within the Stateflow chart nor using Simulink Functions within an expression are  affected by this bug.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink computes incorrect bounds for variable size signal and concat operation from MATLAB Function block
Description: Simulink tries to compute the size of a variable-size matrix in a concat  operation.? This produces incorrect code if a variable size matrix is concatenated with a static size matrix. An example of? MATLAB code in a MATLAB Function Block that induces this error is:   function P2   = fut_ans_mismatch  coder.varsize('P2', [35 30], [1 1]);  P1 = randi([0 0]);  P2 = [ones([12 20 1 1]); ones([12 20 1 1]); ones([P1 20 1 1]); ];

Summary: Simscape crash when using flexible subsystem variants
Description: Simscape models can crash at simulation when using flexible subsystem variants.
Workaround: When using subsystem variants with Simscape models, ensure that all variants have consistent connection port names and positions, and that the variant subsystem has correspondingly consistent port names and positions.

Summary: Model fails to update because Simulink cannot derive input port dimensions of a Merge block from its output port dimensions.
Description: Your model can have this problem when it contains a Merge block with all of the following conditions:  Signal dimensions have not been specified for any of its input signals. For example, all inputs are grounded or connected indirectly to root Inport block whose Dimensions property is specified as -1. Signal dimension has been specified for its output signal. For example, the output signal is connected directly to an Outport block with Dimensions property explicitly specified (not equal to -1).  The Merge block lacks the logic to back propagate output signal dimension to its input signals. Consequently, the input port dimensions are left unspecified until Simulink applies heuristics to derive the input signal dimensions. Such heuristics may result in input dimensions that are not consistent with the previously specified output dimensions, resulting in an error during block diagram update.
Workaround: Move the dimension specification from the output signal of the Merge block to one of its input signals. Otherwise, you must ?use Signal Specification blocks to specify dimensions for all input signals to match the expected output port dimensions.?

Summary: Word output does not honor even and odd page header and footers specified programmatically
Description: Microsoft Word does not honor even and odd page headers and footers set programmatically by a DOM API client. Word honors only the first page and default headers and footers set by the client.
Workaround: You can use your report's Word template to enable different even and odd page headers and footers that are created programmatically.To create a copy of the default DOM Word template in the current folder, at the MATLAB command prompt, enter:mlreportgen.dom.Document.createTemplate('mytemplate','docx'); Open the new template in Microsoft Word.Double-click any page header or footer area in the template to display the Headers & Footers Tools tab in the Word ribbon.Select Different Odd and Even Pages in the Headers & Footers Tools tab.Close the template.Run your DOM-based document generation program.

Summary: Dependency analysis does not pick up certain class files
Description: Classes that inherit a static method or property of a parent class are not picked up by dependency analysis if MATLAB code depends on that class only through the inherited static method or property. For example, ClassA  is the parent class and is defined as: classdef ClassA < handle    methods(Static, Access='public')        function DoWork()            disp('Doing Work!');        end    end    end ClassB  is the child class and inherits from ClassA , thus inheriting the static method DoWork  as well. classdef ClassB < ClassAend Now a function called testFunction()  depends on ClassB  by calling the static method DoWork . function testFunction()    ClassB.DoWork();end In this case, dependency analysis performed by matlab.codetools.requiredFilesAndProducts , mcc  or deploytool  will not pick up ClassB.m  as a dependency.
Workaround: Add the class manually as a dependent file by using the %#function pragma in the MATLAB code at the top, like so:function testFunction()%#function ClassB    ClassB.DoWork();end

The following code snippet provides an example: void model_step() {?  int my_local_i32;?  ...?  #if VARIANT ?   {?  ? ? int my_local_i32;  ? ? ...?  }?  }?  ?

Summary: A function that calls inputname can error with the id MATLAB:inputname:notInAfunction if called directly by a function compiled with MATLAB Coder
Description: A function that calls inputname  and is called directly by a function that is compiled using the MATLAB Coder can generate an error with this ID: MATLAB:inputname:notInAfunction  This error only happens when you execute a Simulink model with the play button.  It occurs because the inputname  function does not find any code on the MATLAB stack to look for names in. If you run the model with the sim  command, the error does not occur because the call to sim  is found on the stack.
Workaround: Call the function via a helper function that is annotated with coder.extrinsic. The function that calles inputname must also be coder.extrinsic.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: C++ class in generated code overwrites initial values of tunable parameters for a reference model inside a reusable subsystem
Description: The overwrite (setting to zero) of initial values of tunable parameters for a reference model inside a reusable subsystem occurs when all of the following conditions apply: A parent model contains a reusable subsystem which contains a referenced model. For the referenced model, code generation is configured using the C++ class as interface. The referenced model has tunable parameters with nonzero initial values. For the parent model, the Remove internal data zero initialization  parameter value is not selected or the setting does not exist.  Note that this issue also affects Simulink Coder starting in R2014a.
Workaround: There are multiple ways to stop code generation of the parent model from re-initializing the tunable parameters for a reference model inside a reusable subsystem.EitherSelect the Remove internal data zero initialization parameter (if applicable).OrMove the referenced model outside of the reusable subsystem.

Summary: DOM WhiteSpace format fails in HTML output
Description: ?Setting a paragraph's WhiteSpace  property to 'normal'  fails. For example, this script   import mlreportgen.dom.*  d = Document('g1330531', 'html');  p = Paragraph(sprintf('  Hello  \n\nWorld'));  p.WhiteSpace = 'normal';  append(d, p);  close(d);  rptview(d.OutputPath); generates HTML output in which the words "Hello" and "World" are on separate lines. The 'normal'  setting should cause a browser to ignore the initial spaces in the text, collapse the white space between the two words,?and ignore the line feeds in the output text.? This behavior occurs because the?DOM API replaces spaces in HTML text with non-breaking spaces and converts?text with?line feeds to span elements separated?by HTML br  elements. This was done originally?to ensure correct white-space formatting in the MATLAB HTML browser, which did not handle white space correctly in the DOM API's initial release. MATLAB's current browser handles white space correctly, so this workaround is no longer necessary and is being eliminated. If you are upgrading from a pre-R2016a release, you must update your report program to use the WhiteSpace format to preserve?white-space formatting ?in HTML output.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Division operations specified with saturation on integer overflow and nontrivial scaling incorrectly replaced with division implementation that wraps on integer overflow
Description: If any of the following conditions are true, a code replacement library (CRL) can cause the code generator to incorrectly replace a division operation for saturation on integer overflow and nontrivial scaling with a call to an implementation function that wraps on integer overflow: An input or output type of the division operation has a nonzero bias. Net slope adjustment factor (net-saf ) is not equal to 1, where net-saf  is calculated as numerator_saf /(output_saf *denominator_saf ) .

Summary: Simulation of models containing a generated S-function block can miss zero-crossing detection
Description: Simulation of models containing a generated S-function block can miss zero-crossing detection under the following conditions: The model configuration enables zero-crossing detection. For example, the configuration sets the Solver  parameter value to 'Variable-step'  and sets the Zero-crossing control  parameter value to 'Enable all'  or 'Use local settings' . The model contains a generated S-function block. The code for the block is generated from a model or a subsystem using the S-function target (rtwsfnc.tlc ).

Summary: mexcuda command can use the wrong version of the NVIDIA compiler on Windows
Description: On Windows, mexcuda  will use whichever version of the NVIDIA CUDA compiler is first on the system path, even if you have explicitly specified its location using MW_NVCC_PATH. Typically this is the version of the most recently installed CUDA Toolkit. If you recently installed an older version of the Toolkit than required by your version of MATLAB, you may find that compilation fails. If you installed a more recent version, the behaviour is undefined - compilation may succeed but there is a small chance of a runtime error during execution of your mex function. You can check which version of the CUDA compiler is going to be used by mexcuda  using the following command   !nvcc --version The version displayed needs to match the ToolkitVersion  displayed when you run the gpuDevice  command.  If this bug applies to you this will be version 7.0.
Workaround: Manually add the compiler location to the beginning of the system's PATH environment variable before running mexcuda. If you have installed the Toolkit in the default location, the following code will work for you:   cuda_bin_dir = 'C:\Program Files\NVIDIA GPU Computing Toolkit\CUDA\v7.0\bin';  setenv('PATH', [cuda_bin_dir ';' getenv('PATH')]);

Description: If you connect to an OPC UA server using OPC Toolbox, and the server restarts without notifying connected clients that it is shutting down and restarting, MATLAB will crash. If the server shuts down but does not restart, MATLAB consumes large amounts of system memory until it is closed.

Summary: Assignment of unzoned datetime to datetime array with timezones is incorrectly allowed
Description: Assignment of unzoned datetime values into elements of a datetime array with timezones is incorrectly allowed. Operations combining zoned and unzoned datetimes should error. For example, create a datetime array and specify a timezone. >> d = datetime(2015,1,1,0:2,0,0,'TimeZone','Europe/Vienna')'d =    01-Jan-2015 00:00:00   01-Jan-2015 01:00:00   01-Jan-2015 02:00:00 Assign a datetime without a timezone to the first element of the array.  >> d(1) = datetime(2015,1,1,0,0,0)d =    01-Jan-2015 01:00:00   01-Jan-2015 01:00:00   01-Jan-2015 02:00:00 The assignment should result in an error but instead is allowed. Furthermore, the unzoned datetime is assumed to be in UTC and is converted to the 'Europe/Vienna' timezone, leading to d(1)  having a time of 01:00:00 .
Workaround: Explicitly specify timezones before assigning them to an array that is already zoned.  Avoid combining zoned and unzoned datetimes.

Summary: Compiling or running a system containing a Xilinx Zynq-Based Radio transmitter or receiver block without the correct environment setup can crash MATLAB.
Description: Compiling or running a Simulink model containing a Xilinx Zynq-Based Radio transmitter or receiver block without first setting up the correct environment settings can crash MATLAB. The featured examples that ship with the product contain a callback on the info block that performs this operation automatically. If you use your own models and run or compile a system without performing the environment setup, MATLAB will crash.
Workaround: Make sure the environment is set up correctly before running or compiling a model. The example below is for PicoZed SDR; you can modify it for your  hardware.>> dev = sdrdev('PicoZed SDR');>> setupSession(dev);To automate the environment setup, put this code in the model's initialization callback.

Summary: Compilation errors from missing TargetLink include folders
Description: If you use TargetLink 3.5 or later to generate code, Polyspace requires additional include folders to verify your code. If these include folders are missing from the Polyspace project, the compilation phase will fail. For example, if your Polyspace project does not include the file tllimits.h , your verification will fail.

Summary: Creating an array of structures with inconsistent field name order results in incorrect value assignment
Description: In MATLAB R2015b, if you create an array that contains structures with the same field names in a different order, the fields are assigned incorrectly. This incorrect assignment occurs only if the structure contains more than two fields. Consider the following example. L.a = 'a'; L.b = 'b';L.c = 'c';  R.b = 'b'; R.c = 'c'; R.a = 'a';  L(2) = R; Because the field names are in different order, the values for L(2)  are assigned incorrectly. L(2)ans =     a: 'c'    b: 'a'    c: 'b'
Workaround: Ensure that the fields of each structure assigned to the array are created in the same order.

Summary: Error when creating distributed array with 58 or more workers
Description: When a parallel pool has 58 or more workers, then attempting to create a distributed  array from the MATLAB client using the following syntax:    d = distributed(x); will fail with an error.
Workaround: You can use an spmd block to create the distributed array as follows:   spmd    d = codistributed(x);  endAfter executing this code, d will be a distributed array.

Summary: Regenerating code from AUTOSAR model might change order of RTE API calls for receiver ports
Description: Regenerating code from an AUTOSAR model might change the generated order of RTE API calls if both of the following conditions exist: The model contains receiver ports that are used for AUTOSAR sender-receiver communication. The Simulink inports mapped to the AUTOSAR receiver ports are configured for ExplicitReceive  or QueuedExplicitReceive  data access mode.  Under these conditions, the generated order of RTE API calls for the receiver ports might differ from the previous build.
Workaround: To work around this bug, preserve the AUTOSAR model build folder between builds. Subsequent rebuilds can use incremental code generation, which avoids unnecessary regeneration of source code whenever possible.

Summary: HTML document part template library not accessible from Report Explorer's document conversion template manager
Description: You cannot edit an HTML template's document part template library using the Report Explorer's document conversion template manager.
Workaround: To edit an HTML document conversion template's document part template library:Start MATLAB.Change to the directory containing the document conversion template.At the MATLAB command prompt, enterunzipTemplate TEMPLATE_NAME.htmtx;delete TEMPLATE_NAME.htmtx;TEMPLATE_NAME is the name of the document conversion template. These commands unzip your template into a directory named TEMPLATE_NAME. The directory contains the template's document part template library file, which is named docpart_templates.html.Open the template library file in the MATLAB editor or the text editor of your choice.Edit and save the file.At the MATLAB command prompt, enterzipTemplate TEMPLATE_NAME;rmdir TEMPLATE_NAME s?

Description: Standalone applications that call pyversion  fail when run on a computer without MATLAB installed.
Workaround: Copy the contents of the folder $MCRROOT\mcr\toolbox\matlab\external to a new folder, $MCRROOT\toolbox\matlab\external.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails if model uses Simulink Enumerated data that are not zero-indexed and/or non-consecutive
Description: If a Simulink model uses a Simulink Enumeration with values that are not zero-indexed and/or non-consecutive, then the Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails and reports the following error:  Translation failed: Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: util_prim/cg_assert.cpp line 38: aIndex>=0 && aIndex  An example follows:  % Simulink Enum with non zero-indexed and non-consecutive values used in random orderclassdef Fruit < Simulink.IntEnumType??? enumeration??????? Apple(1)??????? Banana(5)??????? Coconut(2)??????? Dates(4)??? endend
Workaround: Ensure zero indexed and consecutive enumeration values as the below example:classdef Fruit < Simulink.IntEnumType??? enumeration??????? Apple(0)??????? Banana(1)??????? Coconut(2)??????? Dates(3)??? endend

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: atan2 function with fixed-point input can return incorrect values
Description: The atan2  function, which is implemented using a lookup table, returns incorrect values when the input is fixed point and it reaches the last value stored in the lookup table. This can occur when the two inputs are nearly identical. For example, the following produces an incorrect result:  >> y = fi(25000,1,32,0);>> x = fi(25001,1,32,0);>> atan2(y,x)ans =     0.9623          DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: binary point scaling            Signedness: Signed            WordLength: 16        FractionLength: 13  The correct answer in double-precision floating point is the following: >> atan2(25000,25001)ans =    0.7854
Workaround: Replacing atan2  with cordicatan2  for fixed-point inputs will produce correct results.  For example:>> y = fi(25000,1,32,0);>> x = fi(25001,1,32,0);>> cordicatan2(y,x)ans =     0.7854          DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: binary point scaling            Signedness: Signed            WordLength: 32        FractionLength: 29

Summary: FIR Decimation, FIR Interpolation, Two-Channel Analysis Subband Filter, and Two-Channel Synthesis Subband Filter blocks cannot maintain input rate for sample-based input.
Description: If the Input processing  parameter of the FIR Decimation block, FIR Interpolation block, Two-Channel Analysis Subband Filter block, or Two-Channel Synthesis Subband Filter block is set to Elements as channels (sample-based)  without first setting the Rate options  parameter to Allow multirate processing , the following error message appears: Cannot maintain input rate for sample-based input.
Workaround: Change the Input processing parameter to Columns as channels (frame based) and click Apply.Change the Rate options parameter to Allow multirate processing and click Apply. Change the Input processing parameter to Elements as channels (sample based) and click Apply.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using a session with counter input channels
Description: When using an NI DAQ device, and a data acquisition session with one or more counter input channels, clocked operations (startForeground  and startBackground ) can cause MATLAB to crash or hang. Here is an example program that can result in this behavior: s = daq.createSession('ni');addCounterInputChannel(s, 'Dev1', 0, 'EdgeCount');addAnalogInputChannel(s, 'Dev1', 0, 'Voltage');data = startForeground(s);  ?
Workaround: Apply the patch from bug report 1277716.?

Summary: Slide with background color does not display content
Description: Adding content to a slide that has a background color has no effect. For example, this code creates a slide based on a layout named Title and Content  defined in a template named MyTemplate . The layout has a red background color and the presentation opens in Windows. import mlreportgen.ppt.*slides = Presentation('MyPresentation','MyTemplate');add(slides,'Title and Content');replace(slides,'Title','Agenda');close(slides);winopen(slides.OutputPath); This code results in a presentation that has a single empty slide with a red background. ?
Workaround: Instead of setting the background color of the layout:Insert a rectangle that covers the layout and has the background color you want.Send the rectangle to back to expose the other objects in the layout.

Summary: Comments in Truth Tables using non-ASCII characters are corrupted when saved to MDL format

Workaround: The corruption on the comments happens when saving the model to MDL format.  MDL models created in previous releases are not affected by loading them. Saving those models to SLX format avoids the problem.

Summary: Particular sequence of type cast conversions may cause Embedded Coder to crash during code generation
Description: The following modeling pattern may cause Embedded Coder to crash during code generation: A block has a single output that feeds into a Data Type Conversion block. On the Data Type Conversion Block Parameters dialog box, the Output data type  parameter is set to uint8 . The Data Type Conversion block feeds into another block. The output of this block has a data type of uint16.   ?
Workaround: 1) On the Optimization pane, deselect the Remove code from floating-point to integer conversions that wraps out-of-range values parameter.or2) On the Data Type Conversion Block Parameters dialog box, change the Output data type parameter from uint8 to uint32. Insert a Bitwise And block after the Data Type Conversion block. On the Bitwise And block parameters dialog box, set the Bit Mask parameter to 255.
