Summary: sfclose does not work
Description: sfclose  is a documented API intended to let users close a chart by name from the command line. However, the command has no effect, and does not throw an error.
Workaround: To close a chart from the command line, do the following:r = sfroot;c = r.find('-isa', 'Stateflow.Chart', 'Name', 'MyChart');c.visible = 0;

Summary: S-function with variable sample time might error in rapid accelerator simulation
Description: If you try to simulate a model which has an S-function block with a variable sample time in rapid accelerator simulation mode, in rare cases you might encounter an error during the simulation run similar to this: Error occurred while simulating the model 'test' in rapid accelerator mode. In the model 'test' the variable sample time for tid 3 has a timeOfNextSampleHit (0.01) which is smaller than the current time (0.01) This error can occur at a different time depending on the numerical characteristics of your model. The model should simulate without any errors in normal and accelerator simulation modes. This flaw is exposed only when rapid accelerator timing engine adjusts the next hit time of the S-function variable sample time by a few multiples of double precision epsilon around the current simulation time. The timing engine makes this adjustment to avoid an extremely small time step in the simulation which can cause undesirable numerical behavior and is a rare occurrence for most simulations.

Summary: Inconsistent output of pwelch between releases
Description: In R2015b, calls to pwelch  can return a one-sided PSD estimate, instead of a two-sided estimate as in previous releases. For example, consider a complex vector with zero imaginary part:  >> s = complex(rand(1000,1),0);>> [pxx,f] = pwelch(s,[],[],1024,10);   In R2015a, this code returns a two-sided PSD estimate. In R2015b, the exact same call returns a one-sided PSD estimate.
Workaround: To ensure that pwelch produces two-sided output, use the 'twosided' option: >> [pxx,f] = pwelch(s,[],[],1024,10,'twosided');

Summary: Checkng links from DOORS module can cause error "Unrecognized issue type: ..." error in rmi('checkdoc') utility
Description: When using the rmi('checkdoc')  utility to locate inconsistent links form IBM Rational DOORS to Simulink, you can see an error like this: Error using rmiref.DocChecker/checkLinks (line 56)  Unrecognized issue type: [Simulink reference
Workaround: Follow these steps to use the attached patch to avoid this issue.Unzip the attachment.Run MATLAB as Administrator, to make sure you can write in installation directory,Change MATLAB current working directory to the extracted subfolder and run the toolboxPatch.m script with no arguments:Rerun RMI setup script using this command: rmi('setup')Select the correct path to DOORS installation when prompted in command window, this will install the updated version of dmi.inc under your DOORS installation folder.Restart MATLAB as normal user.Rehash toolboxcache.Restart DOORS, login.Retry consistency checking for a DOORS module.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for loops that access the same matrix sections
Description: Incorrectly generated code might occur for MATLAB code that contains loops that modify the same sections of a matrix using different index accesses. This bug might also occur for Simulink code generation of a model that results in the generated C code below. For example, consider the following MATLAB code that contains a copy to matrix A  from matrix B  and C  within a for  loop as follows: function A = memcpy_example_1318579(A,B,C)  ? ? for i=1:10  ? ? ? ? A(i) = B(i);  ? ? ? ? A(i+5) = C(i);  ? ? end  end?  There is an overlap in the access of A[6...10] . The incorrectly generated code contains two distinct memcpy functions of the following form:? ? memcpy(&A[0], &B[0], 10U * sizeof(double));  ?memcpy(&A[5], &C[0], 10U * sizeof(double));  The correctly generated code is as follows:? ? for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {  ? ? A[i] = B[i];  ? ? A[i + 5] = C[i];  ? }  To reproduce this issue, run 'run_memcpy_example_1318579.m'  file in the attached examples in? 'example_1318579.zip' .
Workaround: Set?Use memcpy for vector assignment?to?Off, or, set the?Memcpy threshold (bytes)?parameter to a very high value such as?int32(intmax).

Summary: Clicking link from MATLAB Help browser to web page requiring authentication displays 401 Unauthorized message
Description: Clicking a link to a web page that requires authentication in the documentation results in the error message: 401 Unauthorized . ?

Summary: Closing Polyspace Bug Finder or Polyspace Code Prover and repening it can cause a no available license error
Description: On Linux and Mac OS systems, after closing Polyspace Bug Finder or Polyspace Code Prover, when you open it again and run an analysis, you can get an error message like the following: Error: No Polyspace Bug Finder license available License checkout failed.License Manager Error -4 Maximum number of users for PolySpace_Bug_Finder_Engine reached.Try again later. To see a list of current users use the lmstat utility or contact your License Administrator.Troubleshoot this issue by visiting: https://www.mathworks.com/support/lme/R2015b/4Diagnostic Information: Feature: PolySpace_Bug_Finder_EngineLicense path: [many paths to license file ]Licensing error: -4,0. You must reboot the system to free the license for another analysis.

Summary: Simulating a model that contains a commented out MATLAB System block crashes MATLAB
Description: MATLAB crashes while simulating a model when all  of the following conditions are met:- The model contains MATLAB System block.- The MATLAB System block has mask drawing commands which depends on mask initialization.- The MATLAB System block is commented out. The crash displays the following stack trace: Stack Trace (from fault): [ 0] 0x00007fd37fb69601 SLCompBlockPlatformInfo::getCompBd(SLBlock const*) const[ 1] 0x00007fd37febcbd2 slCreateAndRegisterEvalInfo(SLBlock*, int, SLPrmDesc const*, std::basic_string<char16_t, std::char_traits<char16_t>, std::allocator<char16_t> > const&, SlArray**, AST**) [ 2] 0x00007fd378493d4f SLMask::UpdateMaskWS(unsigned int, bool*) [ 3] 0x00007fd378494095 SLMask::UpdateMaskWS(unsigned int) [ 4] 0x00007fd3784ef6d5 EvalDisplayCmdList(SLBlock*) [ 5] 0x00007fd37848a35c SLMask::DrawIcon(SLBlock*, WinRec_tag*, mwrect const*) const[ 6] 0x00007fd37fb4498e smDrawIcon(SLBlock*, WinRec_tag*, mwrect const*) [ 7] 0x00007fd37fa306d1 BlockDrawIcon(SLBlock*, WinRec_tag*, mwrect const*)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch between FFT/IFFT HDL Optimized validOut signal and dataOut signal for non-contiguous validIn
Description: This issue affects the FFT HDL Optimized block, IFFT HDL Optimized block, dsp.HDLFFT System object, and dsp.HDLIFFT System object.  In Simulink or MATLAB, non-contiguous validIn results in contiguous and correct validOut and dataOut.  In the generated HDL code, non-contiguous validIn results in non-contiguous validOut that does not correspond to the correct dataOut cycles.
Workaround: Design your system to provide contiguous frames of data to the input of the FFT or IFFT.In Simulink, to consolidate a non-contiguous input frame into a contiguous frame, use the FIFO4FFT block, from the FIFO4FFT_lib provided here. Place this block in front of the HDL Optimized FFT block or the HDL Optimized IFFT block. Include the FIFO4FFT block when you generate HDL code for your design.

Summary: Incorrect IO type of "Output" can be generated for input port interface in the IP Core Workflow
Description: In the IP Core Workflow, any interface that is defined as an "INOUT" InterfaceType by the reference design will always result in an "Output" type IO pad being generated regardless of the type of port that interface is mapped to in the Interface Table. This will result in an error during synthesis of the design.
Workaround: The work-around is to change 'InterfaceType' to 'IN', or 'OUT' in the reference design plugin_rd.m file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect analysis results reported for multi-rate and multi-tasking models
Description: For multi-rate and multi-tasking models, Simulink Design Verifier may return incorrect analysis results under either of the following model configuration settings: Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Sample Time >?Multitask rate transition ?is set to 'None' . Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Sample Time >?Multitask rate transition ?is set to?'Warning' and Configuration Parameters > Solver >?Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is unchecked.  Under such configurations, the generated test case or counter example may not satisfy the objective during simulation or the reported design range may be incorrect.?

Summary: Signal Specification block configured for nonbus signals can crash Simulink if the block input is a bus signal
Description: Signal Specification blocks that meet both  of these conditions can cause Simulink to crash during simulation: The block specifies nonbus type (it has its Data type  field set to a value other than Bus: <object name>  or inherit  ). The input signal for the block is a bus signal.
Workaround: If you want a nonbus signal at the output port of the Signal Specification block, add a BusToVector block immediately before the Signal Specification block.If you want to specify that the bus elements should be of a specific type, use a bus object to specify the properties of the elements in the bus signal. Set the Signal Specification block Data type parameter to Bus: <object name>.

Summary: Cannot replace a PowerPoint picture with another picture
Description: The replace  method of a PowerPoint API Presentation  or Slide  object silently fails to replace a Picture  object. The picture does not appear in the output presentation. Summary: Error "IL INTEGRITY ERROR: expr should have ty record"
Description: Polyspace Bug Finder can fail with the following message (or similar): @FFT: IL INTEGRITY ERROR: expr should have ty record(size=32, ptr =(deref=T1 data=T11 )){offset_in_obj: o, size_in_bytes: size, id_ptr: o, last_index: l, ptid: o, nip_or_null: nip_null, offset: o}: L1t An internal consistency problem has been detected.In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information, the computation has been halted.
Workaround: Note: The workaround for this error depends on the code provided to Polyspace Bug Finder.Try disabling the automatic stubs for the standard functions strcpy and strlen. Using the Preprocessor definitions option (-D at the command-line), add two macros: __polyspace_no_strlen and __polyspace_no_strcpy.If step 1 does not fix the error, try disabling the stubs for all the standard functions. You may lose precision because regular automatic stubbing replaces the specific stubbing made for standard functions. Using the Preprocessor definitions option (-D at the command-line), add the macro POLYSPACE_NO_STANDARD_STUBS.If neither workaround solves the problem, contact MathWorks support.

Summary: Missing LSR-xxx Polyspace? Code Prover? tests
Description: 1. DO Qualkfication Kit Polyspace Code Prover Tool Operational Requirements  "Table 2 Semantic Checks" references ISO/IEC 9899:1990. It should also reference ISO/IEC 9899:1999. qualkitdo_codeprover_tor.docx / pdf  2. The DO Qualkification Kit does not include the following tests for the Language Specific Requirements (LSR-xxx): LSR-300: Semantic Reference Model for C Polyspace Code Prover LSR-301: Semantic Support Levels for C Polyspace Code Prover LSR-301-01: backwards goto statements and a goto from the outside of a loop to one the inner instruction shall have SSL3 support. LSR-301-02: threads/tasks with parameters shall have SSL3 support. LSR-301-04: global/static arrays, strings, unions or structs greater than 128 Mb shall have SSL3 support. LSR-301-05: varargs functions without prototype shall have SSL3 support. LSR-301-06: the library functions setjmp(), longjmp(), signal(), raise() and free() shall have SSL4 support. LSR-301-07: dynamic task/thread creation shall have SSL4 support. LSR-301-08: dynamic construction of functions shall have SSL4 support. LSR-301-09: implicit aliasing using absolute addresses shall have SSL4 support. LSR-301-10: arithmetic on function pointers shall have SSL4 support. LSR-301-11: a function which returns a pointer to a local variable shall have SSL4 support. LSR-301-12:, the following functions cannot be redefined as they are hard-coded in Polyspace Code Prover: - malloc, calloc, realloc, valloc, alloca, __builtin_alloca LSR-301-13: Compatible structure passed in arg of old KandR function style generates a compilation error, due to Polyspace Code Prover being stricter during compilation than other compilers. LSR-301-14: Flexible size array shall have SSL4 support. LSR-301-15: Arguments of printf-like standard functions shall have SSL2 support. LSR-301-16: Header and source file names with non-ASCII characters shall have SSL3 support. LSR-301-17: Absolute addresses containing objects of pointer types shall have SSL4 support. LSR-301-18: Non-initialized source memory area in the memory copy and move routines (memcpy, memmove) shall have SSL4 support. LSR-301-19: An invalid pointer is considered equal to a null pointer. LSR-301-20: Taking the address of critical section routines shall have SSL3 support. LSR-301-21: Nested block of depth greater than 1000 shall have SSL3 support. LSR-301-22: Call to the main program or routines defined as entry point shall have SSL3 support. LSR-303-01: An arithmetic conversion produces a result that cannot be represented in the space provided, subcase 1: integral types LSR-303-02: An arithmetic conversion produces a result that cannot be represented in the space provided, subcase 2: floating and integral conversions LSR-303-03: An arithmetic conversion produces a result that cannot be represented in the space provided, subcase 3: floating conversions LSR-303-04: An arithmetic operation is invalid (such as division or modulus by 0) or produces a result that cannot be represented in the space provided (such are overflow or underflow) LSR-303-05: For a function call without a function prototype, the number of arguments does not agree with the number of parameters. LSR-303-06: For a function call without a function prototype, if the function is defined without a function prototype, and the types of the arguments after promotion do not agree with those of the parameters after promotion. LSR-303-07: If a function is called with a function prototype and the function is not defined with a compatible type. LSR-303-08: A function that accepts a variable number of arguments is called without a function prototype that ends with an ellipsis. LSR-303-09: A pointer to a function is converted to point to a function of a different type and used to call a function of a type not compatible with the original type. LSR-303-10: A pointer to a function is converted to a pointer to an object. LSR-303-11: A pointer to an object is converted to a pointer to a function. LSR-303-12: A pointer that does not behave like a pointer to an element of an array object is added to or substracted from. LSR-303-13: Unless ... the result points to an element of the same array object, the behavior is undefined if the result is used as an operand of the unary * operator LSR-303-14: An expression is shifted by a negative number or by an amount greater than or equal to the width in bits of the expression. LSR-303-15: The value of an uninitialized object that has automatic storage duration is used before a value is assigned. LSR-303-16: NA The value of the object allocated by the malloc() function is used before a value is assigned LSR-303-17: The value of a function is used, but no value was returned. LSR-303-18: A library function argument has an invalid value, sub case: pointer arguments. LSR-303-19: The assert(int expr) macro puts diagnostics into programs. When it is executed, if expr evaluates to false (...) then the assert macro writes information about the particular call that failed (...) it then calls abort. LSR-303-20: An argument to a character-handling function is out of the domain (ctype.h) LSR-303-21: The locale.h library. LSR-303-22: For all functions, a domain error occurs if an input argument is outside the domain over which the mathematical function is defined. LSR-303-23: A range error occurs if the result of the [mathematical] function cannot be represented as a double. LSR-303-24: Non-local jumps: the setjmp() and longjmp() functions. LSR-303-25: The signal() and raise() functions. LSR-303-26: The varargs.h library. LSR-303-27: The stdio.h library. LSR-303-28: The stdlib.h library. LSR-303-29: The string.h library. LSR-303-30: The time.h library.  ? ?

Summary: MATLAB? can crash when multiple, variable-size arrays with different upper bounds are passed to the same function
Description: In certain cases, MATLAB can crash during code generation when multiple, variable-size arrays with different upper bounds are passed to the same function. The crash file contains this message: SEGV: Assertion at lowermatrixtypesforbackend.cpp line 651 The following example reproduces this problem. This example requires the DSP System Toolbox? software. Write the function myfcn .  function y = myfcn(n)%#codegenassert(isa(n, 'double'));hDFT = dsp.FFT();s = zeros(1,n);y = subfcn(s,hDFT);function y = subfcn(s,hDFT)a = abs(step(hDFT,s(1:228)));b = abs(step(hDFT,s(1:240)));y = a(1) + b(1);  Generate code for myfcn . codegen myfcn
Workaround: Try to unite the variable-size arrays that have different upper bounds into one variable-size array that has an upper bound that is large enough for all of the arrays. In the previous example, use coder.varsize to define a a variable-size array t with an upper bound of 240. Instead of passing  s(1:228) and s(1:240) to abs, pass t to abs.function y = myfcn(n)%#codegenassert(isa(n, 'double'));hDFT = dsp.FFT();s = zeros(1,n);y = subfcn(s,hDFT);function y = subfcn(s,hDFT)coder.varsize('t', [1 240]);t = s(1:228);a = abs(step(hDFT,t));t = s(1:240);b = abs(step(hDFT,t));y = a(1) + b(1);

Summary: CWTFT2 uses or lists incorrect parameter values for five wavelets
Description: CWTFT2  uses or lists incorrect parameter values for the following wavelets: 'dogpow' , 'cauchy' ,'escauchy' , 'isodog' , and 'dog2' .  For the  'dogpow'  wavelet, CWTFT2  uses the incorrect defaults and explicitly specifying the parameters does not result in the correct wavelet being used.  For the 'cauchy'  and 'escauchy'  wavelets, the CWTFTINFO2  help lists the parameters in the incorrect order. The 'sigma'  parameter should precede the 'L'  and 'M'  parameters. Specifying the parameters in the order listed in the help, results in the incorrect wavelet being used. For the 'isodog'  and 'dog2'  wavelets, the help lists an extra unnamed parameter. The help should read {'alpha',1.25} .

Summary: CGIR assertion: aType->ordinalIsInRange(aOrdinal) failed when using different enumerated types in the specification for Data port indices and the control port in Multiport Switch block
Description: In the Multiport Switch block, the CGIR assertion aType->ordinalIsInRange(aOrdinal)  fails under the following conditions. You specify different enumerated data types for the Data port indices  (E1) and the control input port (E2) of the block. The enumerated value of E2 in the control port is out of the range of the value of the Data port indices  parameter. You build the model and run the generated code  Simulink is supposed to throw out a data type mismatch error in this case, but it asserts as aType->ordinalIsInRange(aOrdinal)  instead.
Workaround: The enumerated data type in the control input port must be the same as the enumerated data type in the Data port indices parameter.

Summary: The cameraPose function may return an incorrect camera location and orientation.
Description: In some cases the camera orientation and location returned by the cameraPose function may be incorrect.
Workaround: To avoid the problem in the future, download the attached cameraPose.zip file and follow these steps:1. Uncompress the zip file.2. Copy the cameraPose.m file into the (MATLABROOT)\toolbox\vision\vision folder. (MATLABROOT) can be obtained by running the matlabroot function.3. If prompted whether to replace existing files, click yes.4. After copyting the file, restart MATLAB.After this change, the cameraPose function will work correctly.

Summary: Simulation results may differ between normal mode and accelerated mode
Description: Simulation results may differ between normal mode and accelerated mode for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains two instances of a subsystem that has the Function packaging  parameter set to Reusable function . The two subsystem instances execute at different rates.
Workaround: When simulating the model in accelerated mode, set the Function packaging parameter to Nonreusable function or Inline.

Summary: Assigning to a variable using colon indexing in an element-wise expression can corrupt the output array
Description: If you assign to a variable using colon indexing in an element-wise expression, your output array could be corrupted.  For example, each of the following code blocks should result in an array A of size 2x2x2. % snippet 1A = ones(2,2,2);A(:) = -A(:);whos A  % snippet 2A = ones(2,2,2);C = reshape(A,8,1);A(:) = -C;whos A  However, both of these cases result in corrupted output. Due to the incorrect size of the output array, this corruption can result in an error when you try to use the array.  For example, the following code results in a ˇ°Matrix dimensions must agreeˇ± error. B = A + rand(2,2,2); Other errors resulting from array corruption can include ˇ°Out of Memoryˇ± and ˇ°Maximum variable size allowed by the program is exceeded.ˇ±
Workaround: If you are using colon indexing in an element-wise expression, assign the result of the expression into a temporary variable.  Then, you can use colon assignment.  For example, in the following code, the output is not corrupted.A = ones(2,2,2);temp = -A(:);A(:) = temp;whos A

Summary: VideoReader reads frame orientation incorrectly from some MOV and MP4 files on Mac OS X?
Description: Sometimes, data read by the VideoReader  function on Mac OS X is in the wrong orientation. This is because of a difference in how orientation was encoded by the recording device and the way in which the VideoReader  interprets it. For example, consider a video recorded using a mobile device held vertically. The proper height of the rendered output is 1920 pixels and the width is 1080 pixels. However, VideoReader  incorrectly reports this video has a width of 1920 pixels and a height of 1080 pixels.

Summary: Access violation can occur when using Microsoft Visual C++ 2010 Professional (or earlier) or Windows SDK 7.1 as the compiler for Stateflow or MATLAB Function blocks
Description: An incompatibility exists in using Microsoft Visual C++ 2010 Professional (and earlier) or Windows SDK 7.1 to compile Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks that use large variables for Simulink simulations. This incompatibility can cause MATLAB to produce an Access Violation error, and crash. This issue occurs when the following conditions are both true: The generated code for simulations contains any single C structure that exceeds 2 GB.  This is likely to occur if the aggregate size of the local variables used in any single Stateflow chart or persistent variables in any single MATLAB Function block exceeds 2 GB, or if debugging is turned on and the local variables are larger than 400 MB. A MEX file is generated for the charts or the MATLAB Function blocks in the model. Starting in R2015a, MEX files are created only when JIT compilation mode is not used.  The first condition can be detected by running the attached findLargeDataUsage  script. >> clear mex Delete the slprj folder and the *_sfun.mex* file(s) in your working directory >> findLargeDataUsage('modelName')
Workaround: Do at least one of the following:Use Microsoft Visual C++ 2012 Professional or later versions from the supported compiler list for your releaseReduce the aggregate size of the chart local variables used in a single Stateflow chart or persistent variables in a single MATLAB Function blockYou can also try disabling debugging (in R2014b) or removing all breakpoints (in R2015a or later) in all Stateflow charts and MATLAB Function blocks in your model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Semaphore protection for rate transition buffer access might be incorrect in generated code
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect semaphore protection logic for a rate transition buffer when all of the following conditions are true:   The sample rate transition is a faster block driver a slower block.  The Rate Transition block is configured with :  ?Ensure data integrity during data transfer  block parameter selected.  ?Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)  block parameter is cleared.    Model configuration parameter Allow tasks to execute concurrently on target  is cleared.   For example, in the generated code for the attached model (SemaphoreProtectedRateTransition.slx), the semaphore access logic of function SemaphoreProtectedRateTransition_step2  in SemaphoreProtectedRateTransition.c  is nonfunctional. The variable SemaphoreProtectedRateTransi_DW.RateTransition8_semaphoreTaken ?does not control access to code.  ?This issue does not affect single-tasking execution.
Workaround: Take one of these actions:?Identify the affected?Rate Transition block. Then,?configure the output signal?of that block with global storage (for example, apply a custom storage class to the signal).Generate code that runs in single-tasking mode by?clearing model configuration parameter Treat each discrete rate as a separate task.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generation of memcpy for certain for-loops that write to and read from the same array
Description: In some cases where a generated for -loop writes to and reads from the same array, the memcpy  optimization can generate code that produces a wrong answer. In these cases, a for -loop writes to an array. Code that reads an element of this array depends on the value of the element before the execution of the code that writes to the element. Suppose that the memcpy  optimization generates a memcpy  for the code that writes to the array and places the memcpy  before the for -loop. Because the memcpy  writes to all elements of the array before the for -loop executes, the code in the for -loop that reads the array can produce a wrong answer. The following example illustrates this issue. In the following pseudocode, The statement M(i) = N(i)  writes to M . The statement A(i) = M(i + 1) + X(i + 2)  contains an arithmetic operation that reads M . This operation depends on the value of the element M(i + 1)  before the next iteration of the for -loop writes to this element. for(i = 0,1,100)   M(i) = N(i);   A(i) = M(i + 1) + X(i + 2); end If the optimization replaces M(i) = N(i)  with a memcpy  outside of the loop, the generated code can look like: memcpy(&M[0], &N[0], 100U * sizeof(real_T));for (i = 0; i < 100; i++) {  A[i] = M[i + 1] + X[i + 2];} The memcpy  call writes to all elements of M  before the for -loop executes. Now, the value of M[i + 1]  is the value after the copy from N[i + 1] instead of the value before the copy. ? ? ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, disable the memcpy optimization.For MATLAB Coder:At the command-line, set EnableMemcpy to false.In the MATLAB Coder app, set Use memcpy for vector assignment to No.For Simulink Coder:On the Optimization > Signals and Parameters pane, clear the Use memcpy for vector assignment check box.?

Summary: Fixed-Point Designer incorrectly checks out a DSP System Toolbox license key.
Description: When you create a fi variable or call the isfi function, if both Fixed-Point Designer and DSP System Toolbox are installed, a license key for DSP System Toolbox is incorrectly checked out. A workaround is available:
Workaround: 1)Close MATLAB.    2)Open the file manager for your computer and move the source file isfi.m from ˇ®matlab/toolbox/dsp/dsp/ˇŻ to ˇ®matlab/toolbox/dsp/dsputilities/ˇŻ.    3)Start MATLAB.    4)Reset the toolbox cache by typing the following in the MATLAB     command prompt:        >> rehash toolboxreset        This command makes sure that the file isfi.m can be found in the   new location.

Summary: Incorrect last-modified time and date for files copied into the Current Folder browser
Description: When you copy a file into the Current Folder browser from an outside application, MATLAB? incorrectly sets the file's last-modified date and time to the current date and time. For example, this occurs when you drag and drop a file from Windows Explorer into the Current Folder browser. This issue also appeared in R2010b through R2013a, inclusive. See Bug Report ID 914984  for more details.

Summary: Saved Dashboard blocks, such as Knobs and Switches, do not remain in sync with the connected block parameter
Description: In a saved model with connected Dashboard blocks, such as a Knob or a Switch, if the source block parameter is changed, then the Dashboard block does not get updated. However, if the Dashboard block is tuned, then the source block parameter updates correctly. As a result, if there are two or more saved Dashboard blocks that are connected to the same block parameter, and one of them is tuned, then the other Dashboard blocks do not remain in sync.

Summary: Invalid warning when Outport blocks of atomic or virtual subsystems are driven by signal objects with initial values
Description: Invalid warnings are seen for Outport blocks belonging to atomic or virtual subsystems when all the following conditions are satisfied:  The parameter UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection  of the model is set to Simplified  The signal line connected to the Outport block has a Simulink.Signal object with a valid Initial value  The warning tells the user that the Initial Value specified in the Signal object shall be ignored and overwritten by the Outport block in question. This is not a valid warning since Outport blocks belonging to virtual or atomic subsystems cannot set the 'Initial output' parameter. This can be done only by Outport blocks belonging to conditionally-executed subsystems (like Enabled Subsystem, Function-Call Subsystem etc). It is important to note here that the above warnings will continue to be thrown for Outport blocks of conditionally executed subsystems since such Outport blocks have valid "Initial output' fields and will overwrite the Signal Objects' initial values (even if the 'Initial output' field is left empty).

Summary: CAN Transmit and CAN Receive blocks behave incorrectly with multiple Vector virtual buses
Description: If you have multiple Vector virtual channels configured on your host computer and attempt to use CAN Transmit and CAN Receive blocks over CAN buses other than 'Vector Virtual 1', the messages are transmitted and received over the 'Vector Virtual 1' bus regardless of channel settings.

Summary: Unexpected error (MATLAB:UndefinedFunction)  on Polyspace Job Monitor
Description: Launching an analysis in batch mode you can get an error message at the end of the compilation: Error: Remote execution has failed .On the Polyspace Job Monitor, you can see the job with another error message:  Unexpected error (MATLAB:UndefinedFunction) . Sometimes Polyspace does not release license after closing interface leading to such error messages. ?
Workaround: Installation instructionsOn the server side, log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default this is in c:\Program Files\MATLAB or in /usr/local/MATLAB on Linux systems. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. polyspace is one of the principal sub-folders of <MATLAB>.On the server stop MDCS Server. Summary: Error using classification model exported from Classification Learner
Description: If you exported a classification model from Classification Learner to the workspace and wrote a script to make predictions with new data in R2015a, when you try to run the same code using R2015b you see errors like the following:  Error using predict (line 84) Systems of struct class cannot be used with the "predict" command.  This error occurs because the classification models exported from Classification Learner changed from a classification object in R2015a to a structure in R2015b. The new structure contains a classification object and a new predict function. The new structure allows you to make predictions for models that include principal component analysis (PCA).
Workaround: To fix your code from R2015a to work with classifiers exported in R2015b, change predict to trainedClassifier.predictFcn, where trainedClassifier is the name of your struct variable. For example, change this R2015a code: yfit = predict(trainedClassifier,T{:,trainedClassifier.PredictorNames}) To this R2015b code: yfit = trainedClassifier.predictFcn(T)Supply the data T in same data type as your training data used in the app (table or matrix).If you supply a table, ensure it contains the same predictor names as your training data. The predictFcn ignores additional variables in tables.If you supply a matrix, it must contain the same predictor columns or rows as your training data, and no response variable or other unused variables.Note that the default name trainedClassifier increments every time you export to avoid overwriting your classifiers, e.g., trainedClassifier1. Make sure your code uses the correct name of your struct variable.You can also extract the classification object from the exported struct for further analysis (e.g., trainedClassifier.ClassificationSVM, trainedClassifier.ClassificationTree, depending on your model type). Be aware that if you used feature selection such as PCA in the app, you will need to take account of this transformation by using the information in the PCA fields of the struct.

Summary: The MATLAB Editor and Command Window print using a larger than expected font
Description: The font size of a printed document from MATLAB Editor and Command Window is different under different DPI scaling setting in Windows. For example, font size 12 under 150% DPI looks like 18 under 100% DPI.
Workaround: Downscale the font size in Page Setup to get the expected printing result. For example, under 150% DPI, the font size can be set to 8 in order to produce the same result of 12 under 100% DPI.

Summary: MATLAB crashes on Mac when windows are minimized to dock and certain screen events occur
Description: When one or more MATLAB windows (desktop, figure, etc.) are minimized to the dock, MATLAB might crash if a screen-level event occurs. A screen-level event is something that affects everything the screen displays, such as (but not limited to):  Switching users The monitor or system going to or waking up from sleep Changing screen resolutions Adding/removing monitors  The generated stack trace always includes a line that mentions Java_sun_java2d_opengl_OGLRenderQueue_flushBuffer . For example:  [ 8] 0x000000011ce0084a /Applications/MATLAB_R2015b.app/sys/java/jre/maci64/jre/lib/lwawt/liblwawt.dylib+00030794 Java_sun_java2d_opengl_OGLRenderQueue_flushBuffer+00001594  While the crash is most likely to occur with minimized windows, there may be a rare possibility for it to occur even if no window is minimized. If you experience this, please contact MathWorks Technical Support  and let us know what windows you had open and what might have triggered the crash.
Workaround: Follow the instructions below to install a patch to work around this issue in R2015a or R2015b. If you still experience a crash after installing this patch, please contact MathWorks Technical Support and let us know what windows you had open and which were minimized. Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Macintosh systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R2015b.appExit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > GeneralMake a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Type the following in a terminal window:unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Include the .app extension in the folder name, e.g. unzip -l /Applications/MATLAB_R2015b.app/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Type the following in a terminal window: cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Again, include the .app extension in the folder name.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change log:    Jan. 12, 2016: attachment_1299108_15b_2016_01_08.zip uploaded for 15b    Jan. 22, 2016: attachment_1299108_15a_2016_01_08.zip uploaded for 15a

Summary: Simulink Project can issue an incorrect Simulink license error unless you start Simulink first
Description: If you have not already started Simulink, then Simulink Project can wrongly report that it cannot get a Simulink license, with an error like the following: Unable to check out a Simulink license?  License checkout failed.?  License Manager Error -6   No port number in license file and "FlexNet" service does not exist. ?
Workaround: To avoid this problem, before using Simulink Project, enter load_system('simulink') at the MATLAB command-line, or open the library browser.

Summary: Corrupted SVN Simulink project when trying to rename with only a change of capitalization
Description: When using Simulink Project with SVN source control, you cannot make case-only renames of files or folders. If you try, the name reverts to the original name, but you see modified files in the project. If you try to change the case by changing the name to something else, and then back to the original name with a change of capitalization, you can corrupt your project. Creating an intermediate file like this creates an incorrect status and might remove files from source control. If you commit changes and then run project checks, you see errors.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, commit an intermediate file or folder name change, then commit the rename with the case-change.Rename the file to some intermediate name, and commit that change. For example. rename foo.txt to tempfoo.txt.Rename back to the original name with the change of capitalization, and commit again. For example. rename tempfoo.txt to Foo.txt.??

Summary: Dead code may be generated by Switch or Multiport Switch block
Description: Simulink may generate dead code for unused data input branches of Switch or Multiport Switch block if all following conditions are met. Inline parameters optimization (Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and Parameters ) is on. The control port of a Switch or Multiport Switch block is directly driven by a Constant block. The data input branches of the Switch or Multiport Switch block have the same dimension. The data input branches have the same data type as the output of Switch or Multiport Switch block. The Constant value  parameter of the Constant block is a non-tunable scalar (literal or variable) of Simulink built-in data types. The Constant block is not inside a conditionally executed subsystem.  One particular case is when an Environment Controller block is used in a model. This block relies on the optimization of generated code in Switch and Multiport Switch blocks. As a result of this bug, Simulink might generate code for both simulation and code generation branches.

Summary: compose fails if an input argument contains a symbolic diff expression.
Description: The compose  function fails if one or both input arguments contain a symbolic diff  expression. For example: syms f(x) g(x)f_g = compose(f, g)df_g = compose(diff(f),g)Error using symengine (line 59) The variable is invalid.  ?
Workaround: The only workaround is to compose the functions manually:syms f(x) g(x)f_g = compose(f, g)df_g = symfun(subs(diff(f(x), x), x, g(x)), x)df_g(x) =subs(diff(f(x), x), x, g(x))

Summary: Encoding MIL-STD-1553 bus-controller mode codes does not work correctly
Description: The MIL_STD-1553 block set does not correctly encode the bus controller message for a MIL-STD-1553 mode code. For the three mode codes that should send a word to the remote terminal, the blocks encode the messages as T commands instead of R commands and do not send the word. For regular data word messages, the blocks encode and send the messages as specified.

Summary: Accelerated simulation of masked For Each subsystem causes crash or incorrect output values
Description: Accelerated simulation (including rapid accelerator) can yield incorrect output values or a crash if all of these conditions are true: The model contains at least two For Each subsystems (for example, ForEach_A  and ForEach_B ). ForEach_A  and ForEach_B  iterate more than once during a simulation run. At least one block inside ForEach_A  uses a mask parameter that is partitioned by ForEach_A  or by a parent For Each subsystem. None of the blocks inside ForEach_B  use partitioned mask parameters. In the generated acceleration code, the global structure that stores block parameter values places the values intended for ForEach_B  immediately following the values intended for ForEach_A . In the generated header file, the definition of the structure type appears like this example:  struct P_myModel_T {   P_ForEach_A_myModel_T ForEach_A_p[4];   P_ForEach_B_myModel_T ForEach_B_p;}; In this case, the substructure ForEach_B_p  appears immediately following the substructure ForEach_A_p . Each substructure stores the block parameter values for the corresponding For Each subsystem.

Summary: Error opening model with VR Sink block that links to virtual world file with .WRL or .X3DV extension
Description: You cannot open a Simulink model that includes a VR Sink block that links to a virtual world file with a file name that uses the .WRL  or .X3DV  (in capital or mixed-case letters) extension. ?
Workaround: In the file system, rename the virtual world file, replacing the .WRL extension with .wrl (lowercase) or the .X3DV extension with .x3dv.You do not need to update the file name specified in the VR Sink block Source file field.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Nested if statements with identical conditions containing function calls with side effects may be incorrectly combined into one if statement in the generated code
Description: Simulink Coder may generate code that incorrectly combines nested if  statements that meet the following conditions: The if  statements contain identical conditions. The conditions contain calls to functions that have side effects such that each call can produce a different result.  For example, the if  statements in the code below may be incorrectly merged into one if  statement: Code before merging if  statements: if (rollDice()==7) {  printf("found seven once");  if (rollDice()==7) {    printf("found seven twice");  }}  Code after merging if  statements: if (rollDice()==7) { printf("found seven once"); printf("found seven twice");}  The above if  statements should not be combined in the generated code because rollDice()  may produce a different result with each function call. For example, for the attached model, the code generator assumes that the value of a  is constant and shrinks multiple if  statements with calls to this function to a single function call. As a result, there is no code for braches A2 , A3 , A4 , and A5 .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder may generate incorrect code for an AUTOSAR model containing a conditionally-executed subsystem
Description: Embedded Coder may generate code that contains an uninitialized local variable for an AUTOSAR model that meets all the following conditions: The model contains a conditionally-executed subsystem which contains a Switch block. The Switch block connects to the Outport block of the conditionally-executed subsystem which connects to a Root Outport block. For the root Outport block, the data access  mode is set to ExplicitSend . You set the data access  mode by selecting Code>C/C++>Configure AUTOSAR Interface . For the root Outport block, the Data type  parameter is set to Enum: <class name> . The Data type  parameter is in the Signal Attributes  tab of the root outport dialog box.  ?
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the conditionally-executed subsystem and the Root Outport block. In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, select Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: Performance degradation with NVIDIA graphics boards
Description: On computers running Microsoft Windows, equipped with NVIDIA graphics boards, and using NVIDIA graphics driver version 344 and higher, rendering performance may be significantly degraded. This is caused by a compatibility issue between the rendering engine and newer versions of the NVIDIA driver.
Workaround: Use one of these approaches to workaround this issue:Downgrade NVIDIA graphics driver to version 343 or lower. This has no adverse effects on system performance, but older drivers may be no more available for the installed operating system.Switch to software OpenGL immediately after start of MATLAB by issuing opengl software as the first command. This method somewhat decreases performance of MATLAB graphics. It has no effect on the rest of the system.Change the default method of computing Z-buffer information to a method that is somewhat less exact, but faster, and does not have the problem. To do this, type the command vrsetpref DefaultFigurePlaneClipMethod bvolume. After doing this, some complex virtual worlds may have minor rendering defects but they will render at maximum speed. This workaround is available only for R2015a and R2015b. After upgrading to R2016a, it is recommended to revert to the original default method by typing vrsetpref DefaultFigurePlaneClipMethod factory.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect root outport initialization code generated for AUTOSAR model with branched IRV signal
Description: Embedded Coder can generate incorrect code for an AUTOSAR model that meets all of the following conditions: The AUTOSAR model contains two runnables connected by an inter-runnable variable (IRV). The IRV signal that connects the two runnables also branches to a root Outport block. The root Outport block is configured with any AUTOSAR data access mode. The runnable subsystem that drives the branched IRV signal does not provide an initial condition. That is, the Outport block in the runnable subsystem does not specify a value for the block parameter Initial output  (InitialOutput ).  Under these conditions, Embedded Coder generates code that sets the output using an uninitialized local variable.
Workaround: To work around this bug, in the Outport block of of the runnable subsystem that feeds the root Outport block, specify a value for the block parameter Initial output (InitialOutput).

Summary: Transmit parity selection does not work correctly with CEI-530 and CEI-830 boards
Description: The three transmit parity settings in the CEI-530 and CEI-830 are 'none' , 'odd'  and 'even' . The driver does not use these settings correctly to program the board. Selecting 'none'  parity results in 'odd'  parity, selecting 'odd'  or 'even'  parity results in 'even'  parity.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. Summary: Saving a figure using File > Save As ... resets figure text to default font size and style
Description: When you use File > Save As ...  to save a figure, the font properties reset to the default values. The change in font properties affects both the original figure and the generated output. For example, the title of the following figure uses a large font size. When saved to an image file using File > Save As ... , the title for both the original figure and the image output changes to the default size: surf(peaks);title(gca,'Test','FontSize',20);
Workaround: Use the saveas or print commands from the MATLAB command line to save the figure in the desired format. For example, the following saves the current figure to the test.png file: surf(peaks);title(gca,'Test','FontSize',20);saveas(gcf, 'test.png');

Summary: MATLAB can crash after loading MAT files that contain serialized tcpclient objects
Description: If you load a MAT file containing a serialized tcpclient  object, or one contained in a another data structure, then clear all, MATLAB will crash. For example: >> t = tcpclient('www.mathworks.com',4012)>> save('repro.mat')>> clear all>> load repro.mat>> clear all The generated stack trace begins with: [  0] 0x000000002cf42970 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015b\bin\win64\asynciocore.dll+00010608 asynciocore::Channel::isOpen+00000000[  1] 0x000000002cdbd2cb C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015b\bin\win64\asyncioimpl.dll+00119499[  2] 0x0000000010dcfbf6 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015b\bin\win64\mcos_impl.dll+00130038

Summary: Unknown code template compliance level error when using TLC
Description: Using the Code Template API or SFunction TLC API might cause code generation to fail with the following error message:Unknown code template compliance level: 0
Workaround: To workaround this issue, prior to using the Code Template API or SFunction TLC API, set the LibSetCodeTemplateComplianceLevel function to Compliance level 1 as follows: LibSetCodeTemplateComplianceLevel(1)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results for fixed-step simulation of models with continuous states that contain a Rate Transition block with continuous input
Description: Results might be incorrect for fixed-step simulation of models that contain continuous states (e.g., model contains blocks from the Continuous blocks library) and that have a Rate Transition block being driven by a continuous signal. This issue occurs because the Rate Transition block incorrectly participates in the ODE integration phase.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes if you run a Simulink model that contains more than one model reference to a model that contains Vision HDL Toolbox blocks.
Description: This issue occurs when both the following conditions are true: Your model has more than one model reference to a model that contains Vision HDL Toolbox blocks and those Vision HDL Toolbox blocks have ports of pixelcontrol bus type. You simulate in "Normal" simulation mode.
Workaround: Set the simulation mode of the referencing model to Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator.Save the model and close it. (This step clears cached variables relating to the bus data type.) Reopen the model and run your simulation.

Summary: MISRA C++: false positive on rule 4-5-2
Description: When checking MISRA-C++ rule 4-5-2: "Expressions with type enum shall not be used as operands to built- in operators other than the subscript operator [ ], the assignment operator =, the equality operators == and !=, the unary & operator, and the relational operators <, <=, >, >=.", Polyspace may raise a false positive when there is no operator violation.  Example: ... = MyFunction(the_parameter); //Violation 4-5-2 There is no operator on the line where the violation is raised. ?
Workaround: A cumulative patch exists. Please follow this link to download it:https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/1292111???

Summary: innerjoin and outerjoin error for empty table inputs
Description: The innerjoin  and outerjoin  functions error if one of the inputs is a table with no rows. For example, >> t1 = table([1;2;3],rand(3,1),'VariableNames',{'Key','X'})t1 =? ? ?Key? ? ? ?X? ? ? ?___? ? _______? ? 1? ? ?0.81472? ? 2? ? ?0.90579? ? 3? ? ?0.12699>> t2 = table(zeros(0,1),zeros(0,1),'VariableNames',{'Key','Y'})t2 =? ? empty 0-by-2 table>> innerjoin(t1,t2)Error using table/innerjoin (line 83)With two inputs, the first argument must be a vector. Use three-input syntax for matrices.
Workaround: If one of the input tables could have no rows, check for that before calling innerjoin or outerjoin. For example,if height(t2) > 0 ? ?t3 = innerjoin(t1,t2)else? ?t3 = [t1([],:) t2([],'Y')]end

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for integralImage with 'rotated' orientation
Description: Matlab Coder and Simulink Coder can generate incorrect code for the integralImage  function using the rotated  orientation option. J = integralImage(I, 'rotated')
Workaround: Disable the EnableMemcpy optimization in a coder configuration, then use this configuration to generate code for the integralImage function.>> cfg = coder.config('mex'); >> cfg.EnableMemcpy = 0;>> codegen -args {I, coder.Constant('rotated')} -config cfg

To see the contents of the .zip file: Type the following in a terminal window :unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name, e.g. unzip -l /Applications/MATLAB_R2015b.app/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Summary: Dell users can see the Simulink error "Can't reload $MATLABROOT\bin\win64\sl_graphical_classes.dll" or a MATLAB crash when saving or opening files
Description: The interaction between MATLAB and Dell Backup and Recovery 1.8 versions of Qt5Core.dll  can cause one of these problems: MATLAB crashes when displaying the File Open or File Close dialog boxes, if the MATLAB version of the Qt5Core.dll  loads before the Dell version. Simulink fails to run and displays this error message, if the Dell version of Qt5Core.dll  loads before the MATLAB version:         Error: Cannot reload $MATLABROOT\bin\win64\sl_graphical_classes.dll  MuPAD fails to run and displays an error message like this:         Invalid MEX-file 'matlabroot\toolbox\symbolic\symbolic\mupaduimex.mexw64':       The specified module could not be found.
Workaround: Windows 10 users can update to Dell Backup and Recovery 1.9 or later.For other Windows versions, either uninstall Dell Backup and Recovery or disable its shell extensions. To disable the Dell Backup and Recovery shell extensions, use a utility such as http://www.nirsoft.net/utils/shexview.html to selectively enable or disable these shell extensions:C:\Program Files (x86)\Dell Backup and Recovery\Components\Shell\DBROverlayIconBackuped.dllC:\Program Files (x86)\Dell Backup and Recovery\Components\Shell\DBROverlayIconNotBackuped.dllC:\Program Files (x86)\Dell Backup and Recovery\Components\Shell\DBRShellExtension.dllDisabling Dell Backup and Recovery shell extensions:Removes the backed-up and nonbacked-up icon overlays in Microsoft ExplorerRemoves any context menus on files from Dell Backup and RecoveryMight have other unintended side effectsIf you are affected by this issue, contact Dell support.

Summary: The readtable function truncates long row names
Description: The readtable  function truncates row names longer than the length specified by the namelengthmax  function when reading row names. The function reads row names when the 'ReadRowNames'  name-value pair is set to true . For example, consider the following file called temp.txt : rowName,var1,var2,var3,var4veryLongRowName_[45 characters]_tail,1,2,3,4  t = readtable('temp.txt','ReadRowNames',true);  t.Properties.RowNames = 'veryLongRowName_......_';  The substring tail  is beyond the namelengthmax  limit and is therefore truncated.

Summary: Unable to simulate a Simulink model in Rapid Accelerator, RSim, ERT, or GRT modes on Mac OS X version 10.11 El Capitan
Description: When running MATLAB on Mac OS X version 10.11 El Capitan and attempting to simulate a Simulink model in Rapid Accelerator, RSim, ERT, or GRT modes, an error message such as "Unable to determine if rapid accelerator target started successfully" appears.

Select the root folder of the install, for example, MATLAB_R2015b.app.Enter the name and password of an account with Administrator privileges.When the upgrade is complete, you can re-launch MATLAB and simulate your model.

Summary: The MATLAB? Coder? app can stop responding after generating code for large projects
Description: For some projects with many MATLAB?  files, the MATLAB?  Coder? app can stop responding in the Generate Code  step after code generation completes.
Workaround: You can continue to use the app to configure your project and settings, but use the command-line interface to generate code. To generate code at the command line using a project file, pass the project file as an argument to codegen. For example:codegen myProject.prj

Summary: Warning message appears when a model with Dashboard blocks is simulated if the stop time of the model is set using a variable.
Description: A warning message appears when a model with Dashboard blocks is simulated if the stop time of the model is set using a variable. Signals connected to any Dashboard Scope block in the model will not stream to the Dashboard Scope block. Other Dashboard blocks will continue to stream data. To be notified of additional information about this bug, including when it is fixed, click the Watch  checkmark.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: freqspace(0)?returns NaN instead of an empty row vector
Description: In generated code,  freqspace(0)  returns NaN  instead of an empty row vector (zeros(1,0) ).

Summary: Storage class specification may not be honored on a conditional subsystem outport with no associated signal object
Description: If you specify the storage class for a signal line with no associated signal object connected to a conditional subsystem outport block, the storage class specification is not honored for the following conditions: Outport is driven by a block with non-overwritable output such as a Ground, Constant, or a Stateflow block Outport is connected to the input of a Merge block Outport shares the same signal source with another Outport nested within the current subsystem but having an explicit initial output value of its own Outport shares the same signal source with another Outport in the same subsystem having an explicit initial output value of its own
Workaround: Move the storage class specification to the signal line outside of the conditional subsystem.

Summary: Deployed component GUIs and figures have different look and feel than MATLAB desktop
Description: Deployed GUIs and figures look and feel different compared to undeployed MATLAB desktop versions. Deployed applications are using the cross platform look and feel by default, while the undeployed figures and GUIs use the platform specific look and feel.
Workaround: To work around this issue, manually set the look and feel to the default platform specific version.  If you're using workaround 2 or 3, depending on the platform you're deploying to, use one of the look and feel codes below and apply it with the workaround.  This is not necessary if you're using workaround 1.For Windowscom.sun.java.swing.plaf.windows.WindowsLookAndFeelFor Linuxcom.jgoodies.looks.plastic.Plastic3DLookAndFeelFor Maccom.apple.laf.AquaLookAndFeelWorkaround 1: Applied before deploying, on the MATLAB installationThis workaround is applied to the MATLAB installation on the development machine before packaging.  Any subsequent deployment projects will pick up the change.Download the startup.zip file attached below and unzip it to get the startup.m file.  Place it in a directory that's on the MATLAB path, such as:  C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015b\toolbox\localorC:\Users\username\Documents\MATLABSee the documentation on startup for more information.If you already have a startup.m file, copy the contents of the below file and add it to the beginning of your startup.m file instead.Workaround 2: Applied before deploying, on the MATLAB code to be deployedThis workaround is applied to the MATLAB code that is being deployed, before packaging.  It only affects that particular deployment project.To set the look and feel, add the following line with the appropriate look and feel name to your MATLAB file once prior to creating graphics or figures.javax.swing.UIManager.setLookAndFeel('look_and_feel_code')where look_and_feel_code is the platform specific look and feel code listed at the top.If you're deploying platform-independent Java packages, Python modules or Production Server archives, use the ispc, isunix, or ismac MATLAB code to determine the platform and apply the correct look and feel.The above Java code can fail if Java Swing is not loaded.  Call usejava('swing') to check whether it is enabled or not.Workaround 3: Applied after deployingThis workaround is for the target machine where the MATLAB runtime is installed and it can be applied after deployment.  It requires no MATLAB code change and affects all deployed components that utilize the runtime.Create a text file called java.opts (no .txt extension) with the following contents on one line:-Dswing.defaultlaf=look_and_feel_codewhere look_and_feel_code is the platform specific look and feel code listed at the top.Place the java.opts file in the following directory of the MATLAB runtime installation:mcrroot\bin\archwhere mcrroot is the MATLAB runtime installation location, and the arch is the runtime architecture.  For example,C:\Program Files\MATLAB\MATLAB runtime\v90\bin\win64Alternatively, the java.opts file can also be placed in the startup directory of the deployed component if that directory is known.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when a library has same-named nested subsystems
Description: Opening a library with non-unique subsystem names in the library hierarchy (for example, aLibrary/Subsystem/Subsystem1/Subsystem) causes a crash.
Workaround: Use unique subsystem names in a library hierarchy as block paths looking like potential cycles will be filtered out in the Library Browser view.

Summary: On machines with the AVX instruction set, the singular value decomposition (SVD) of ill-conditioned complex matrices might fail to converge
Description: This issue only occurs on machines with processors that support the Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX) instruction set. When computing the singular value decomposition of ill-conditioned complex matrices, svd  might fail to converge, producing the following error message: >> [U,S,V] = svd(A); Error using svd SVD did not converge.   Computing just the singular values is not affected by this issue: >> S = svd(A);
Workaround: For MATLAB releases R2013b and higher, you can work around this problem by setting two environment variables prior to starting MATLAB. Set MATLAB_DISABLE_CBWR to 1Set MKL_CBWR to SSE4_2 by referring to the following MATLAB Answers article:https://www.mathworks.com/matlabcentral/answers/223482-how-do-i-set-add-or-change-environment-variables

Summary: bwmorph with 'endpoints' returns incorrect results for a specific pixel pattern
Description: The bwmorph  function includes an option called 'endpoints' , which computes the endpoints of connected components in a binary image. This is done using a lookup-table, where all possible 3x3 neighborhoods are pre-computed and stored. This bug affects one specific 3x3 neighborhood pattern, for which the output is true , even though the center of the neighborhood is false .
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Add-On Explorer can be slow to start
Description: The Add-On Explorer can be slow to start when many MathWorks? products are installed. The Add-On Explorer displays a spinner in the open window until the startup process is complete.
Workaround: You can visit Bug Report ID 1296073 to download a patch that addresses this issue.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: System object method processTunedPropertiesImpl?might not get called for a nested object
Description: In certain cases, in generated code, the method?processTunedPropertiesImpl  is not called when a property of a system object is modified if all of the following conditions are true: An object is nested inside another object (that is, an object is a property of another object)? A property of the inner object is modified via a chain of reference. For example:  outerObject.innerObject.property = something;?  The inner object's property being modified is a tunable one?  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: If a call to processTunedPropertiesImpl is important for computing the correct answer, then you can split the assignment that causes the call into two. For example:inner = outerObject.innerObject;?inner.property = something;?

Summary: MATLAB can crash during MEX code generation with variable-size file name argument to fopen
Description: When you generate MEX code from MATLAB code that passes a variable-size file name to fopen , MATLAB can crash with an error message similar to: ValidCore violated: checkCastable: Illegal cast to DynamicMatrix  This error occurs when the file name is a variable-length string and either the MATLAB code contains a parfor  loop or extrinsic calls are disabled.
Workaround: Pass a fixed-size string for the file name argument to fopen. A zero-terminated fixed-size buffer can also be passed for the file name during code generation. For example, create a MATLAB file fopen_buf.m with the contents:function fid = fopen_buf(filename, varargin)if coder.target('MATLAB')    fid = fopen(filename, varargin{:});else    % Allocate fixed size buffers for the file name, zero terminate it, and    % copy the input file name to the buffer.    % Adjust N to suit your needs    N = 512;    N_filename = numel(filename);    assert(N_filename <= N);    filename_fixed = char(zeros(1,N));    filename_fixed(1:N_filename) = filename(:);    fid = fopen(filename_fixed, varargin{:});end Call fopen_buf in place of fopen in your MATLAB code.

Summary: ForEach subsystem code might incorrectly reuse variable from its containing model's code
Description: ForEach subsystem code?might incorrectly reuse a variable from its containing model's code, leading to wrong answers, if all of the following conditions are true: Optimize global data access  configuration parameter is set to Use global to hold temporary results  or Minimize global data access . Enable local block outputs  configuration parameter is enabled. Reuse local block outputs  configuration parameter is enabled. In the ForEach subsystem block parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation  tab, the Function packaging  parameter is set to Auto  or Inline .  The ForEach subsystem variable that gets increased or decreased with every iteration of the for  loop might get?used as an array index after the for ?loop processes.

Summary: Test manager report creation fails if there are Unicode strings in error or log messages
Description: If there are Unicode strings in the Errors  or Logs  sections of test results, then the test manager returns this error if you generate a report: "Invalid character code sequence detected."
Workaround: As a workaround, you can choose to not include error and log messages in the report by clearing the check box Error and log messages in the report creation dialog. You can also work around this problem by generating the report using the English locale setting.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsystem initialization function using memset function may be missing in generated code
Description: Generated code may be missing the subsystem initialize function for block inputs and outputs and DWork vectors when the following conditions are met: On the Subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, the Function packaging  parameter is set to Nonreusable function , the Function Interface  parameter is set to void_void , and the Function with separate data  parameter is set to On . On the All Parameters  tab, the Use memset to initialize floats and doubles to 0.0  parameter is set to On .  For example, for the attached model MissingSubsystemInit.slx , the generated code does not contain a subsystem initialize function.
Workaround: If the generated code does not contain a subsystem initialization function, deselect the Use memset to initialize floats and doubles to 0.0 parameter. The code generator generates a subsystem initialize function that does not contain a memset function.

Summary: fdesign.octave may result in unstable filters for certain center frequencies
Description: fdesign.octave may result in unstable filters if the sampling rate is not large enough to accommodate the selected center frequency. Based on The American National Standards Institute's "Specification for Octave-Band and Fractional Octave-Band Analog and Digital Filters" (ANSI S1.11), the upper passband band-edge frequency of the octave bandpass filter is given by: f2 = (G(^1/(2*b))) * fm, where fm is the midband frequency of the bandpass filter, 1/b is the bandwidth designator, and G = 10^(3/10). If f2 is larger than Fs/2 (where Fs is the filter's sampling frequency), then the designed filter might be unstable and will not meet the desired specifications. The validfrequencies method of fdesign.octave returns center frequencies that abide by the Nyquist limit (i.e. all center frequencies are less than Fs/2). However, validfrequencies did not check that f2 must be less than Fs/2. Certain frequencies returned by validfrequencies may therefore lead to an unstable filter.

Summary: Discover Clusters wizard cannot discover MJS clusters that are incompatible with the client MATLAB
Description: In R2015b, you can no longer discover incompatible MATLAB Job Scheduler (MJS) clusters on the local network using the Discover Clusters wizard. Incompatible clusters run a version different to your version.  You can still discover compatible MJS clusters correctly.

Summary: Xilinx Zynq-Based Radio Support Package: When a targeted DUT is placed in the receive path, connectivity checks fail.
Description: The testConnection method of the device object verifies connectivity with attached hardware. One of the tests verifies that test data (a sinusoid) can be received from the RF chip. However, if a targeted DUT is in the receive path, the sinusoid is corrupted and the test fails. Secondly, the displayed error message itself contains an error that results in the error, "Incorrect number of parameters supplied for 'sdrpluginbase:SdrzFunctions:BISTTestFail'. Expected 0 but found 1."
Workaround: 1. Verify that there is a receive DUT targeted. This example is for PicoZed SDR; you may need to modify it for your hardware.>> dev = sdrdev('PicoZed SDR');>> status = info(dev);>> status.HardwareRxCapabilitiesans =Device has targeted Rx: DUT ChannelMapping = 12. Run testConnection and verify that the "Testing RF antenna loopback" test does not fail. This testpoint has the DUT bypassed correctly. >> dev = sdrdev('PicoZed SDR');>> testConnection(dev);3. Use the attached modified version of the Tone Receiver example instead of the shipping version. This version results in a correctly displayed sinusoid. The file has been modified at lines 101 and 102 to bypass the user DUT in the receive path.

Summary: Incorrect SIL and PIL simulation results for multitasking, reusable function code without generated main program
Description: A model produces incorrect software-in-the-loop (SIL) and processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation results with this combination of settings: Configuration Parameters > Solver > Tasking mode for periodic sample times  set to MultiTasking  Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Interface > Code interface packaging  set to Resusable function  Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Templates > Generate an example main program  set to off   The SIL or PIL application does not correctly call the model step functions in the generated code, which produces incorrect numerical results.
Workaround: Use a generated main program. Set Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Templates > Generate an example main program to on.?

Summary: C++ 03 standard math library can emit with math functions codes that earlier compilers do not recognize
Description: When generating C++ code with C++03 standard from a model, the generated code can call functions that are not defined by C++ 03 standard, such as 'fmaxf ', 'sinf ', 'cosf ', and 'sinhf '. Certain compilers, such as Visual Studio 2010 (or earlier), do not have these functions and generate a compiler error.
Workaround: For the generation of C++ code, go to Code Generation Options > Standard Math Library and choose C89/C90 ANSI. Note: For R2013b and earlier versions of MATLAB, go to Code Generation Options > Code Replacement Library and choose C89/C90 ANSI.

Summary: Pre-R2015a generated S-functions compatibility issue in release R2015a or R2015b
Description: A generated S-function is an S-function binary MEX file (for example, *.mexw64 ) built using the S-function target (rtwsfcn.tlc ). A MATLAB crash occurs in release R2015a or R2015b if you simulate a model containing a generated S-function that was produced using a release before  R2015a if  the generated S-function contains zero crossings and uses a variable-step solver. These generated S-functions from before  R2015a operate normally (no crash) in releases after  R2015b.
Workaround: In R2015a and R2015b, to use a generated S-function that was produced using a release before R2015a when the generated S-function contains zero crossing and uses a variable-step solver, use one of these approaches:In R2015a or R2015b, regenerate the S-function from your model before using the generated S-function in other models.In the older release, regenerate the S-function from your model using a fixed-step solver before using the generated S-function in other models in R2015a or R2015b.

Summary: Updating the name of a test harness belonging to a component in a new model that has not been saved causes MATLAB to crash.
Description: If you create a test harness for a component in a model that has not been saved and has no file on disk, then updating the name of the test harness using the harness properties dialog or sltest.harness.set  will result in a MATLAB crash.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, save the model prior to performing the rename operation.

Summary: CORDIC functions incorrectly flagged as unsupported for code generation in MATLAB Coder and Fixed-Point Converter apps
Description: When using the Fixed-Point Converter app or the MATLAB Coder app with automated fixed-point conversion, CORDIC functions, such as cordicsqrt , are incorrectly flagged as unsupported for code generation. These warnings occur during the "Review Code Generation Readiness" step and the "Convert to Fixed Point" step of the apps.
Workaround: You can ignore warnings that CORDIC functions are unsupported for code generation. The warnings do not prevent code generation from proceeding normally.

Summary: ?Code generation assertion when AUTOSAR model inport with IsUpdated data access mode branches to nonreusable subsystem or Simulink Function
Description: Code generation fails with an assertion if all of the following conditions exist:  An AUTOSAR model contains an Inport block. In the Configure AUTOSAR Interface dialog box, in the Simulink-AUTOSAR Mapping  view, the Simulink inport is mapped to an AUTOSAR port and configured for IsUpdated  data access mode. The Inport block branches, and at least one of the inport destinations is a nonreusable subsystem or a Simulink Function block.
Workaround: To work around this bug:If the IsUpdated signal has destinations that are nonreusable subsystems,?open each subsystem and change Function packaging to Auto or Inline. Alternatively, avoid branching?IsUpdated signals that have nonreusable subsystem or Simulink Function destinations.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Saturation port of Discrete-Time Integrator block can give incorrect results
Description: The saturation port of the Discrete-Time Integrator block outputs 3 possible values ('1.0', '0' and '-1.0') indicating the saturation status of the block's output port. It allows only double , single  or int8  data types. This port can give incorrect results if the block gets output data types from back-propagation that gives an int8  data type to the saturation port but a double  data type to the output port. This happens when:  The block's OutputDataTypeStr  parameter is set to Inherit via back propagation  Input port data type is double  The signal coming out from the output port does not specify any data type The signal coming out from the saturation port has the int8  data type, due to back propagation.
Workaround: If both input and output ports have double or single data type, make sure that the saturation port has the same data type as the output port. If an int8 signal from the saturation port is required, connect the saturation port to a Data Type Conversion block to convert all possible values ('1.0', '0' and '-1.0') from double or single data type to int8.

Summary: Unnecessary generated code for a model that contains an Assignment block inside a For Iterator subsystem
Description: Simulink Coder may generate unnecessary code in the model _initialize  function for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains an Assignment block inside a For Iterator subsystem On the Assignment block parameters dialog box, the Initialize output (Y)  parameter is set to Initialize using input port <Y0> .  The model_initialize  function may also contain an uninitialized local variable that later gets written to.
