Summary: SVG images not rendered in single-file HTML output
Description: A browser does not render SVG images included in a single-file HTML report produced by the DOM API. For example, an HTML browser does not render the SVG image included in the report generated by the following MATLAB script: import mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Document('MyReport','html-file');surf(membrane);print -dsvg membrane.svg;delete(gcf);append(rpt,Image('membrane.svg'));close(rpt);rptview(rpt.OutputPath);
Workaround: This problem arises because the DOM API specifies the MIME type of SVG images incorrectly as svg instead of correctly as svg+xml. You can fix the generated report by replacing the incorrect MIME type with the correct MIME type in the generated report. For example, the following script postprocesses the generated report to replace the incorrect MIME type with the correct MIME type:import mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Document('MyReport','html-file');surf(membrane);print -dsvg membrane.svg;delete(gcf);append(rpt, Image('membrane.svg'));close(rpt);% Fix SVG MIME typefid = fopen(rpt.OutputPath,'r');s = fread(fid,'char=>char');fclose(fid);s = s';s = strrep(s,'data:image/svg','data:image/svg+xml');fid = fopen(rpt.OutputPath,'w');fwrite(fid,s);rptview(rpt.OutputPath);

Summary: MATLAB crashes when calling strread or textread with the wrong number of outputs
Description: MATLAB crashes when calling strread  or textread  with multiple format specifiers and one or fewer output arguments. For example, the following call will cause MATLAB to crash:  a = strread('a b','%s%s'); textread  behaves the same for similar inputs.
Workaround: Call textscan instead.In most cases, the same input arguments produce similar output. For files, textscan requires a file ID created using fopen.Example:Replace[a,b,c] = textread(filename,format);withfid = fopen(filename);D = textscan(fid,format);[a,b,c] = D{:};fclose(fid);If there are any differences in the output of textscan, call strread/textread with the correct number of outputs.Example, use multiple outputs:[a,b] = strread('a b','%s%s');NOTE: Generally, the number of outputs should match the number of format specifiers.

Summary: Legend colors are incorrect in scatter plots
Description: When you plot multiple scatter  plots on the same axes, the legend icons might have the same color even though the scatter  plots use different colors. For example, this code creates two scatter  plots (one blue, one red) on the same axes. Notice that the legend icons are both red: scatter(1:10,1:10);hold onscatter(2:11,1:10); legend('show') Typically, the legend colors are the same when at least one of the scatter plots has any of these properties set to 'flat' : MarkerFaceColor  MarkerEdgeColor
Workaround: To work around this issue, install the patch associated with this Bug Report using these instructions:Download the .zip file attached to this Bug Report and store it in an empty folder.Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): unzip workaround_1283854-15b_2015-09-01.zipLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Start MATLAB. On Windows Systems, be sure to start MATLAB by right-clicking on the program icon and selecting Run as administrator.Change directory to the folder containing the .zip file from above.Run the script: apply_1283854_15b_2015_09_01.m by typing: apply_1283854_15b_2015_09_01into the MATLAB Command Window.Exit and restart MATLAB.You may need to type rehash toolboxcacheat the command prompt to update a stale toolbox cache.If you are unable to install the patch, there are three alternative workarounds:If the CData of each scatter plot is an RGB triplet, then assign the MarkerEdgeColor or MarkerFaceColor of each scatter plot to the value of the CData:s1 = scatter(1:10,1:10);hold ons2 = scatter(2:11,1:10);s1.MarkerEdgeColor = s1.CData;s2.MarkerEdgeColor = s2.CData;legend('show')Assign an RGB triplet to the MarkerEdgeColor or MarkerFaceColor of each scatter plot:s1 = scatter(1:10,1:10);hold ons2 = scatter(2:11,1:10);s1.MarkerEdgeColor = [0 0.4470 0.7410];s2.MarkerEdgeColor = [0.8500 0.3250 0.0980];legend('show')Use this workaround when all the points within each scatter plot are the same color.Call the legend function with two or more output arguments:s1 = scatter(1:10,1:10,[],1:10);hold ons2 = scatter(2:11,1:10,[],26:35);[h, ~] = legend('show');Use this workaround when the points within each scatter plot are different colors.Change logSeptember 9, 2015: workaround_1283854-15b_2015-09-01.zip: initial version

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Call to a persistent function that reads a persistent variable without writing it, and has its own code to error if the persistent variable is uninitialized may produce wrong answers
Description: Incorrect code can result from a function with a persistent variable, designed so that some call sites read the persistent and other call sites write the persistent.? The function must also have explicit checks to report an error if the persistent is not initialized in the calls that read it (for example using assert or error). Here is an example that illustrates this behavior:? function out = exfcn(1);out = fcn();disp('see this?');end function y = fcn(u)persistent p if nargin == 1    p = u;end assert(~isempty(p)); % This assertion causes the wrong behavior.y = p;end To see the wrong behavior: 1. Generate the MEX function. ??codegen ex  2. Run the MEX. ?ex_mex
Workaround: ?Remove the?test ?that the persistent variable is not empty from the code.? ?

Summary: Closing or saving a model that has unsaved changes in the model workspace can crash Simulink
Description: Closing or saving a model crashes Simulink when both  of these conditions are true: The model workspace data source is a MAT-file. The model workspace has changed since the workspace was last saved.
Workaround: In the Model Explorer, select the Model Workspace node for the model. In the dialog on the right, to save or discard the changes to the model workspace, click Save to Source or Reinitialize from Source. Then you can save or close the model without causing Simulink to crash.

Summary: Updating a model containing a nonvirtual bus connected to an Outport block in a conditionally executed subsystem can crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB can crash when both these conditions are met:  - A nonvirtual bus feeds the Outport block of a conditionally executed subsystem. - The Outport block has its Initial output  parameter set to 0  or to a valid numeric MATLAB structure.
Workaround: Place a Signal Conversion block before the Outport block and configure its Output to Virtual bus. Alternatively, in the Outport block, change the Initial output parameter value to [].

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: "Execution function" memory section unexpectedly applied to non-finite data
Description: When you specify a memory section for the generated execution functions of a model (Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Memory Sections > Execution ), if the model code contains non-finite data (required for some blocks and modeling patterns, and enabled through Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Interface > Support non-finite numbers ), the code generator applies the memory section pragma to: The relevant functions (expected). The non-finite data (variables) (unexpected).

Summary: Discrete simulation using the Interpolate option for the Tustin solver results in an error
Description: If your model contains a DC Machine block, and you select the Interpolate  option from the advanced settings for a discrete Tustin solver on the powergui block, your simulation results in this error message: Invalid Simulink object name: power_utile/NoSwitch Discrete InterpCaused by:No block called 'NoSwitch Discrete Interp' could be found.

Summary: MPC controller ignores custom output disturbance models without direct feed-through
Description: When you set a custom output disturbance model using setoutdist , the MPC controller ignores models without direct feed-through when computing the state estimator. For example, create an MPC controller for a plant with one state and one output. plant = ss(0.8,0.2,1,0,0.2);mpcobj = mpc(plant);  Extract the default state estimator from the controller. [L,M,model1] = getEstimator(mpcobj,'sys');   The model used for state estimation, model1 , has two states, including one state for the plant model and one state for the disturbance model. Modify the output disturbance model, and set it as a custom disturbance model. This model has no direct feed-through; that is, its D matrix is zero. outdist = getoutdist(mpcobj);outdist.b = outdist.b*5;setoutdist(mpcobj,'model',outdist);  Extract the new state estimator from the controller. [L,M,model2] = getEstimator(mpcobj,'sys');  In this case, the model used for state estimation, model2 , has only one state instead of two. This is because the bug prevents the controller from recognizing the custom disturbance model when computing the state estimator.

Summary: The functions undistortImage() and rectifyStereoImages() may throw an error for large input images.
Description: The function undistortImage() and rectifyStereoImages() will throw an error if the 'OutputView' parameter is set to 'Full', and the width or the height of the input image is larger than 5000 pixels.
Workaround: To avoid the problem in the future, download the attached CameraParametersImpl.zip file and follow these steps: 1. Uncompress the zip file. 2. Copy the CameraParametersImpl.m file into the (MATLABROOT)\toolbox\vision\vision\+vision\+internal\+calibration folder. (MATLABROOT) can be obtained by running the matlabroot function. 3. If prompted whether to replace existing files, click yes. 4. After copyting the file, restart MATLAB. After this change, the undistortImage and rectifyStereoImages functions will work correctly.

Summary: Search using Simulink Find dialog box with Japanese characters does not work
Description: In Simulink, entering Japanese words in the search criteria of the Find dialog box produces garbled text. Clicking Find  does not produce search results.

Summary: Possible incorrect entry-point function signature when example main and reentrant code are both enabled
Description: It is possible that MATLAB?  Coder? generates the incorrect entry-point function signature when the following conditions are true: The build type is source code, static library, dynamic library, or executable. Example main generation is enabled (by default, example main generation is enabled). Reentrant code generation is enabled. Some entry-point function arguments are large structures or matrices. ?
Workaround: Disable example main generation:At the command line, set the code configuration object property GenerateExampleMain to 'DoNotGenerate'.In the MATLAB Coder app, in the project build settings, on the All Settings tab, under Advanced, set Generate example main to Do not generate an example main function.?

Summary: Results mismatch between simulations in normal and accelerator modes may occur when the input to a Trigonometric Function block with a cos function is an imaginary number
Description: Simulating a model in Normal mode may produce a different result than in Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator modes for the following modeling pattern: The model contains a Trigonometric Function block with the Function  parameter set to cos . The input to the Trigonometric Function block is a complex signal. The real component of the signal has a value of 0 . The magnitude of the imaginary component of the signal is a larger than 89.415 for single data type and 710.476 for double data type.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier displays error on models that are configured to stream signals into the Simulation Data Inspector
Description: On models that are configured to stream signals into the Simulation Data Inspector, Design Verifier displays the following error message:  Unexpected internal error
Workaround: Stop signal streaming before invoking Simulink Design Verifier

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when using nested literal cell arrays in case statements
Description: If?you have a nested literal cell array?in a case  statement in your MATLAB code, the generated code matches any element of the nested cell array with the expression in the switch  statement. MATLAB matches only the top-level elements of the literal cell array with the expression in the switch  statement.  For example,?suppose that?you generate code for: x = 1;switch x    case {{1}}        disp('error');end Running the?generated code will print error . On the other hand, executing this code in MATLAB will not print anything.
Workaround: Assign?the literal cell array to a variable. Use this variable as a case statement. For example, rewriteswitch ...    case {{...}}        ...    ...endas?x = {{...}};switch ...    case x        ...    ...end

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when Constant block and Stateflow Chart write to the same Data Store Memory block
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model that meets all of these conditions: A Stateflow Chart contains a call chain that writes to a Constant block. The Stateflow Chart and the Constant block write to the same Data Store Memory block. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Use memcpy for vector assignment  parameter is selected.  In the incorrect code, the memory location for the Data Store Memory is declared constant (that is, not writeable).
Workaround: Unzip the attachment and save the workaroundForBugReport1279304.p file to a folder on your MATLAB path. Run this script in each MATLAB session before opening your model.

Summary: R2013a model configuration reference can cause Simulink Coder and Embedded Coder to inappropriately use the toolchain approach for compiling generated code
Description: In R2015b, Simulink Coder or Embedded Coder can be directed to use the incorrect build approach, using the toolchain approach when the template makefile approach was actually configured. This incorrect build approach for compiling generated code for the model occurs when both  of these conditions exist: The model uses a configuration reference with a configuration set that was saved into a MAT-file using R2013a. The model uses a custom system target file that expects the build to use a specific template makefile.  Under these conditions, the build can fail because the toolchain approach is selected and it likely not compatible with the custom template makefile that was intended to be used.
Workaround: Using a release that is later than R2013a, use one of the following workarounds.Workaround option 1:1. Use the following commands on the configuration set, which was saved as a MAT-file in R2013a. In these commands, the 'hCS' is a handle to the configuration set in question.set_param(hCS, 'UseToolchainInfoCompliant', 'off');set_param(hCS, 'Toolchain', 'Automatically locate an installed toolchain');2. Re-save the configuration set as a MAT-file.Workaround option 2:1. In the System Target File, if it is not present, add an ActivateCallback to the rtwgensettings structure.2. In this callback, use the following commands. In these commands, the 'hSrc' is a handle to private data that is passed in as an argument to the 'ActivateCallback'.hCS = hSrc.getConfigSet();set_param(hCS, 'UseToolchainInfoCompliant', 'off');set_param(hCS, 'Toolchain', 'Automatically locate an installed toolchain');

Summary: Potential mismatch between simulation and code generation results due to a missing typecast in the generated code
Description: If the following conditions are met, there may be a missing type cast in the generated code: On the Code Style  pane, the Casting modes  parameter is set to Standards Compliant . The input value(s) to a block cause(s) overflow.  The generated code should contain a type cast that truncates the expression that represents the value of the block. The missing type cast may cause a mismatch between simulation and code generation results.
Workaround: Check that the Detect overflow parameter on the Diagnostics > Data Validity pane is set to error. Redesign your model to prevent overflow.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Switch statement?with a?character?switch expression and noncharacter case expressions, or vice versa, may result in a wrong answer in generated code
Description: The generated code can differ from MATLAB for a switch statement with: A?character switch expression and noncharacter case expressions A noncharacter switch expression and character case expressions  ?In these cases, the code generator excludes the case expressions for the nonmatching type, regardless of the value. For example, consider this code: x = 'a';switch x    case double('a')        disp(1);    otherwise        disp(2);end ?In?MATLAB, this code displays 1, but the generated code displays?2. This issue occurs in MATLAB Coder, a MATLAB Function block, MATLAB in Stateflow, and a MATLAB System block..?
Workaround: Make sure that the switch expression and case expressions are either all character or?all noncharacter.??Convert the switch and?case expressions to the larger type, usually the noncharacter type.For example:x = 'a';switch x    case double('a')        y = 1;    otherwise        y = 2;endshould be rewritten as:x = 'a';switch double(x)    case double('a')        y = 1;    otherwise        y = 2;end

Summary: Coverage analysis fails when a referenced model in accelerated mode references other models
Description: If all of the following conditions are true, Simulink displays an error when you attempt to run coverage analysis on a model containing a multi-level hierarchy of referenced models. The Simulation Mode  parameter of a Model block is set to Accelerator The referenced model itself references one or more other models The referenced model is not already opened or loaded when performing coverage analysis  The following error message is displayed:  Input must be a structure.Caused by:    A fatal error occurred when accessing the coverage tool: cvi.TopModelCov.modelStart
Workaround: When working with a multi-level hierarchy of model references, ensure that any model reference which is set to Accelerator simulation mode, and also contains references to other models, is loaded or opened before beginning coverage analysis.

Summary: Copying and pasting commented blocks causes segmentation violation
Description: A segmentation violation can occur when you copy and paste parts of your model that contain commented-out or commented-through blocks.
Workaround: If parts of your model contain commented-out or commented-through blocks, uncomment those blocks before a copy-paste operation.

Summary: On Linux, MATLAB crashes when a path name contains a non-ASCII character and is longer than 1024 bytes.
Description: The presence of non-ASCII characters in a path name, plus an overall length greater than 1024 characters, leads to a condition where MATLAB mishandles character buffer memory while trying to process the path. This results in an "Abort" condition, the termination of MATLAB, and the generation of a SegV report.
Workaround: Either rename path elements containing non-ASCII characters to ASCII-only or shorten the path name so the total length is less than or equal 1024 characters.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for nonvirtual subsystem input requiring data alignment might not be aligned?
Description: If all of the following conditions apply, the code generator can produce code for input arguments that does not include compiler preprocessor directives to align the data to a specific boundary in memory, as required by the target implementation: The model includes a nonvirtual susbsystem that has an input port connected to a buffer outside the subsystem block, such as a Signal Conversion block.  The nonvirtual subsystem includes an operation supported by the code replacement library whose replacement implementation requires inputs to be aligned.  The nonvirtual subsystem port is the source of the input that requires alignment.   If the compiled code from your compiler for the target processor would result in different numeric result or crash when using an instruction?that requires specific data alignments but the data is not aligned properly, you will get incorrect results when the all of the conditions above are met.

Summary: Noise figure calculations for passive RFCKT objects may be incorrect for non-symmetric network data
Description: When creating and analyzing passive RFCKT objects in the RF Toolbox (such as rfckt.passive  or rfckt.datafile ) using non-symmetric network data, the wrong noise figure may be calculated. As an example, consider a simple two-port rfckt.passive  S-parameter network whose S12 = S22 = 0. s_data = zeros(2);s_data(1,1) = 0.527818352575060 - 0.476913236398653i;s_data(2,1) = 0.443437175119063 + 0.226431824733583i;freq = 0;net = rfdata.network('Data',s_data,'Freq',freq);p = rfckt.passive('NetworkData',net);analyze(p,freq);disp(p.AnalyzedResult.NF)  If we analyze  the network p  we expect the correct noise figure (after the patch is installed) to be 6.06 . Instead the returned answer is incorrect, and varies by release and platform.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inconsistent results between simualtion and code generation for LookupTableND and Prelookup blocks for using a mix of floating-point and fixed-point data types
Description: Prelookup and LookupTableND index search and fraction calculation have numerical differences between simulation and code generation. This happens when a mix of floating-point and fixed-point data types are used in the block. For instance, when the input is double or single type and the breakpoints are fixed point, there are numerical differences. This difference does not occur when only fixed point data types are used for the input, breakpoints, and fraction, or when only floating points are used for these.
Workaround: Use only fixed-point or only floating-point data types for the input, breakpoints, and fraction.

Summary: Streaming logged data from Simscape models to Simulation Data Inspector produces an error
Description: Streaming the data from Simscape models to Simulation Data Inspector, by selecting Record logged workspace data in Simulation Data Inspector  check box on the Data Import/Export  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, can produce the following error message:   Physical modeling internal error: fullblockPath can't be shorter than block source.    This error occurs if a block name in the model contains a forward-slash ('/') character.
Workaround: Replace the forward-slash ('/') character in Simscape block names with any other valid character, for example, with an underscore ('_') character.

Summary: Code generation with MINGW compiler not supported for models with XCP or J1939 blocks
Description: If you run a Simulink model with XCP or J1939 blocks from the Vehicle Network Toolbox block library in rapid accelerator mode, you might see the following error: ### Build procedure for model: '<modelname>' aborted due to an error.Unable to build a standalone executable to simulate the model 'modelname' in rapid accelerator mode.Caused by:Error(s) encountered while building "<modelname>":### Failed to generate all binary outputs.  If you try to generate code from the model using the MINGW compiler, you might see the following error message: ### Build procedure for model: '<modelname>' aborted due to an error.Error(s) encountered while building "<modelname>":### Failed to generate all binary outputs.

Description: MATLAB code generated from the sensorArrayAnalyzer App produces the wrong spacing when the Array Type is set to Uniform Linear  or Uniform Rectangular  and when the Element Spacing unit is set to lambda . The generated code assumes element spacing to be in meters instead of lambda.

Summary: intlinprog can return a suboptimal solution
Description: When intlinprog  solves a problem with the CutGeneration  option set to the default 'basic' , or to 'intermediate'  or 'advanced' , it can use flow cover cuts. A bug in the flow cover cut algorithm can cause these cuts to cut off the optimal solution, leading intlinprog  to return a suboptimal solution. To see whether this bug might have affected your optimization, check the iterative display for the phrase 'flow cover cut' . If iterative display was disabled, enable it by setting the Display  option to 'iter' .
Workaround: Disable cut generation by setting the CutGeneration option to 'none' using optimoptions.

Summary: TableColSpec object can fail to format table columns correctly
Description: The DOM API fails to honor the Span  property of a TableColSpec  object. For example, the following script intends to use a single TableColSpec  object to override the style of the first two columns of a table. However, the script succeeds only in overriding the first column. import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('mydoc');t = Table(magic(5));% Specify default column stylet.ColSpecGroups = TableColSpecGroup;t.ColSpecGroups.Style = {Color('green'), Bold, Width('.5in')};% Override style for first two columnst.ColSpecGroups.ColSpecs = TableColSpec;t.ColSpecGroups.ColSpecs.Style = {Color('red')};t.ColSpecGroups.ColSpecs.Span = 2;append(d, t);close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath); A TableColSpec  object can also cause the DOM API to fail to honor a column width specified by its parent TableColSpecGroup  object. For example, the following script intends to specify a column width of 0.5in for all columns. However, the DOM API fails to honor the specified width. import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('mydoc');t = Table(magic(5));t.ColSpecGroups = TableColSpecGroup;t.ColSpecGroups.Span = 5;t.ColSpecGroups.Style = {Color('green'), Bold, Width('0.5in')};t.ColSpecGroups.ColSpecs = TableColSpec;t.ColSpecGroups.ColSpecs.Style = {Color('red')};t.ColSpecGroups.ColSpecs.Span = 2;append(d, t);close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);
Workaround: Create a TableColSpec object for each column whose format you want to specify. For example, the following script colors the first two columns red and the remaining columns green.import mlreportgen.dom.*;d = Document('mydoc');t = Table(magic(5)); % Specify default column stylet.ColSpecGroups = TableColSpecGroup;t.ColSpecGroups.Style = {Color('green'), Bold, Width('.5in')};% Override style for first two columnsspecs(1) = TableColSpec;specs(1).Style = {Color('red')};specs(2) = clone(specs(1));t.ColSpecGroups.ColSpecs = specs;append(d, t);close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);The following script creates .5in  columns and overrides the color of the first two columns.import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('mydoc');t = Table(magic(5));t.ColSpecGroups = TableColSpecGroup;specs = TableColSpec.empty(0,5); % preallocate spec arrayspec = TableColSpec; % create default specspec.Style = {Color('green'), Bold, Width('0.5in')};for c=1:5 specs(c) = clone(spec); % initialize spec arrayend% override first two columns.specs(1).Style = {Color('red'), Bold, Width('0.5in')};specs(2).Style = {Color('red'), Bold, Width('0.5in')};t.ColSpecGroups.ColSpecs = specs;append(d, t);close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath); ?

Summary: Compilation error 'unrecognized token'
Description: During the compilation phase of a C++ analysis, you may see the following error message: "File C:\...\myfile.h line 1 Error: unrecognized token ..."  The verification fails due to a file encoded in UTF-8 format with 'Byte Order Mark' (BOM). For C++ analyses, Polyspace cannot compile BOM bytes.
Workaround: To work around the issue, remove the BOM bytes from the file. Linux: Use the command iconv to change the file encoding to UTF-8 without BOM. Windows: Use a text editor that can handle file encoding, such as Notepad++.

Summary: Block dialog boxes resized or repositioned on Linux
Description: Under certain window managers on Linux, Simulink block dialog boxes can appear in the wrong size or position. Some window managers treat dialog boxes specially for the purpose of sizing and layout. For example, tiling window managers such as i3 override the saved size and position of a top-level window and modify it to use all available screen space. Block dialog boxes were coded like top-level windows, which can cause unwanted dialog box resizing and repositioning.

Summary: S-Function builder does not obtain the correct path when the path itself contains the exact string of the tag
Description: When the S-Function Builder parses the Libraries pane for the tags SRC_PATH , LIB_PATH  and INC_PATH , it removes the tag everywhere in the string in case insensitive form. For example, if you enter a path of the following format in the Library/Object/Source files  field in the Libraries pane: SRC_PATH pathString1/pathString2_src_path/pathString3  it removes the string 'src_path ' and stores the source path as pathString1/pathString2_/pathString3  Similarly, if you use something like path2_inc_path  with the INC_PATH  tag or path2_lib_path  with the LIB_PATH tag, the text corresponding to the tag in the path will be removed. Building the S-function will result in errors relating to missing files or undefined functions.
Workaround: To work around this problem, use a path that does not include the string of the tag it is used with.

Summary: Persistent variables error during simulation or code generation for Stateflow charts with local messages
Description: Stateflow throws an error when you attempt either of the following operations with a chart that contains messages of local scope: Simulate in Rapid Accelerator mode Generate code with External mode  chosen for the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Interface > Data exchange > Interface   The following error message is displayed in the Diagnostic Viewer: Code generation and simulation targets registered different sets of persistent variables for the block. External or Rapid Accelerator mode simulation requires code generation and simulation targets to register the same set of persistent variables for this block. This discrepancy is typically caused by MATLAB functions that have different code paths for simulation and code generation targets where these code paths define different sets of persistent variables. Please identify these code paths in the offending block and rewrite the MATLAB code so that the set of persistent variables is the same between simulation and code generation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation results for Delay Line block and dsp.DelayLine

Summary: Find Files tool unable to search on certain characters.
Description: The Find Files tool is unable to search for text containing characters that are not encoded in UTF-8. If the Find files containing text:  field contains characters that are not encoded in UTF-8, the Find Files tool returns 0 results.

Summary: Data type registration error from custom blocks that use pixelcontrol bus
Description: pixelcontrol is a non-virtual bus data type used by the blocks in Vision HDL Toolbox. If you also use pixelcontrol bus as the data type for custom blocks, such as a MATLAB Function block, within a Simulink model containing Vision HDL blocks, you might see the following error:  Expression 'pixelcontrol' for type of data 'ctrl_in' did not evaluate to a valid type. Datatype error reported from block 'name /SFunction' Cannot register data type 'pixelcontrol' because another type with this name already exists  To register the bus data type, models that include pixelcontrol include evalin('base', 'pixelcontrolbus'); in the model init callback. However, if multiple blocks attempt to register the data type, this evaluation is not always sufficient.
Workaround: Create a renamed copy of the bus object in the base workspace. In model init callback, run the following code:evalin('base','pixelcontrolbus'); mypixelcontrol = pixelcontrol; Then in your custom blocks, such as MATLAB function blocks or bus creators, use only the 'mypixelcontrol' bus.

Summary: Output of perms is not in reverse lexicographic order
Description: In R2013a and later versions of MATLAB, the documentation of perms  states: "perms(v)  returns a matrix containing all permutations of the elements of vector v  in reverse lexicographic order." However, this statement is inaccurate for R2013a through R2015b releases of MATLAB, since perms  does not actually return the output in this order. perms  is updated in R2016a so that it produces output in reverse lexicographic order, as the documentation states.

Summary: Hang when retrieving from an SVN repository on a UNC path
Description: If you try to sync an SVN sandbox located on a UNC path using a file:/ URL, MATLAB can hang if your Java system property "user.dir" also specifies a UNC path.
Workaround: To fix this problem, change your Java system property user.dir to a nonUNC location. UNC locations start with \\. Instead, set the property to a folder that starts with a drive letter. In MATLAB, type:java.lang.System.setProperty('user.dir',[non unc path]) For example, java.lang.System.setProperty('user.dir','C:/myhome').After setting this property, try retrieving the sandbox again.

Summary: Generating a manifest for a model that contains test harnesses can result in an error
Description: If you generate a manifest for a model that has not been changed, and the model contains one or more test harnesses with RebuildOnOpen  enabled, an error is returned stating that the model must be saved first even though the model has not been changed.
Workaround: When generating the manifest, select Allow models with unsaved changes to be analyzed. This option appears in the Generate Model Manifest dialog box after you select Analysis > Model Dependencies > Generate Manifest.

Summary: For some models that do not have floating-point arithmetic, Simulink Design Verifier reports that they do have floating-point arithmetic
Description: When analyzing a model where signal ranges are specified on blocks or Signal objects or Stateflow data objects, the Simulink Design Verifier report contains the following warning, even if the model does not have floating-point arithmetic and no rational approximation is applied: The model includes floating-point arithmetic. Simulink Design Verifier approximates floating-point arithmetic with rational number arithmetic.

Summary: Unable to use fonts in graphics that have Unicode characters in the names
Description: Setting the FontName  property for a graphics object to a Unicode character vector, such as ŁÍŁÓ Ă÷łŻ, does not change the font.

Summary: Parallel building for referenced models fails when static code metrics report is generated
Description: Parallel building for referenced models fails if the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Report > Static code metrics  parameter is selected for at least one of the referenced models. The software generates an error similar to the following: Build folder /tmp/tp71421ff9_26ab_43ef_8692_536131aa50bb/child1/slprj/ert/child2 appears invalid. Please try rebuilding the model.
Workaround: Do not enable the parallel building of referenced models when generating a static code metrics report. For the top model of the model reference hierarchy, in the Configuration Parameter dialog box, on the Model Referencing pane, clear the Enable parallel model reference builds check box.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Memory allocation fails for a variable-size array whose size is between (intmax/2) + 1 and intmax
Description: Suppose that the size of a variable-size array  is between (intmax/2) + 1  and intmax . To increase the size of the array, the generated code tries to double the size of the allocated memory. The memory allocation fails because the doubled size is greater than intmax . For the generated MEX, the memory allocation results in an error. For generated libraries and executables, the memory allocation results in an infinite loop because the error checks are not present. For example, consider the following MATLAB?  program:  function y = fcn(n) assert(isa(n, 'int32')); y = zeros(1, n); Running the MEX when the value of  n  is  intmax/2 + 1  results in an error.  >> codegen fcn >> fcn_mex(intmax/2+1); Cannot grow a matrix with greater than intmax() elements. ?

Summary: Unsaved test harnesses are discarded if you attempt to save a model to a read-only directory
Description: Attempting to save a model to a directory for which you do not have write permission results in the following error: Error using save_system (line 38)Error writing file "C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015b\foo.slx":  copy failed: fl:filesystem:AccessDenied Permission denied  If the model you attempted to save contains test harnesses that were not previously saved, the harnesses are lost.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: No diagnostic message for Simulink model in fast restart mode after change in Variant control value
Description: Tunability of Variant control parameter in fast restart mode is not supported. However, no diagnostic error/warning messages are shown when user attempts to tune the variant control value when model is initialized for fast restart.

Summary: H1 lines with non-ASCII characters do not render properly in the Current Folder Browser.
Description: In cases where a MATLAB Code File containing an H1 line (first help text line of the code file) with non-ASCII characters is selected in the Current Folder Browser, it will not be rendered properly in the Detail Pane.

Summary: Variables editor displays incorrect values for fixed-point variables containing more than 64 rows or columns
Description: When a variable with a fixed-point data type is opened in the Variables editor, row or column numbers 64 and greater do not display properly when scrolled into view. Instead, the Variables editor repeats the display beginning back at row one or column one.

Summary: Moving figures between an Apple Retina display and another display reduces graphical detail
Description: On some multiple-monitor Macintosh systems, graphical quality might decrease when you move figures between displays: If you create graphics on an Apple Retina display, and move the figure to another type of display, some of the graphical detail is lost. You might also notice missing lines or other subsampling artifacts. If you create graphics on a display that is not an Apple Retina display, the graphics look blurry when you move them to an Apple Retina display.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect output initialization for AUTOSAR model with Stateflow chart
Description: Embedded Coder can generate incorrect code for an AUTOSAR model that meets all  of the following conditions: The AUTOSAR model contains a Stateflow chart that connects to a root Outport block. The root Outport block is configured with AUTOSAR data access mode ExplicitSend . The Stateflow output that connects to the root Outport block is conditionally initialized in the Stateflow chart.  Under these conditions, Embedded Coder can generate code that sets the output using an uninitialized local variable.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the conditionally-initialized Stateflow outport and the root Outport block it connects to. In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, select Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: You cannot delete a specific element in an enumeration array using subscripted assignment.
Description: For example, EnumExample  is an enumeration class derived from a numeric class: classdef EnumExample < int32  enumeration     One(1)     Two(2)   endend  If you use subscripted assignment to delete an element of an array of EnumExample  enumerations, MATLAB issues the following error: >> arrayOfEnumExample = EnumExample([1,1,2,2]);>> arrayOfEnumExample(2) = [];Error using subsasgnIn an assignment A(:) = B, the number of elements in A and B must be the same.
Workaround: Workaround this bug by assigning to a temporary variable, deleting the element, then reconstruct the array:>> arrayOfEnumExample = EnumExample([1,1,2,2]);>> temp = int32(arrayOfEnumExample);>> temp(2) = [];>> arrayOfEnumExample = EnumExample(temp);?

Summary: Exporting a Test Harness discards harness model parameters
Description: Exporting a test harness discards the model parameters for the harness model and replaces them with default parameters. Therefore, saved parameters are lost when a test harness is exported to a separate model.  This behavior occurs for both ways of exporting a test harness:Selecting File > Export Model To > Independent Model  Using the sltest.harness.export  function

Summary: Incorrect positioning and clipping of UI components that were saved in character units
Description: When loading a previously saved UI, the location and size of the components inside the UI might differ from the Position  property values of those components when the UI was saved. This issue can occur if both of the following circumstances are true: The system that saved the UI and the system that loaded the UI have different screen resolutions. You change a component's Units  property to 'characters'  after setting its Position  property. UIs created using GUIDE are also affected if you change the Units  property of a component after dragging that component onto the canvas.
Workaround: To prevent the problem:Change the Units property before changing the Position property.Avoid setting the Units property to 'characters'.

Summary: Model block SIL/PIL fails with multirate Data Store Memory block
Description: A Model block software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processer-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation produces an error when the component under test contains a multirate Data Store Memory block that has Storage class  set to a value that is not Auto  or SimulinkGlobal . For example, Storage class  is set to ExportedGlobal . When the Model block parameter CodeInterface  is Top model , you see the following error message: IsExportFcnDiagram && ~UsePortBasedTs When the Model block parameter CodeInterface  is Model reference , you see the following error message: For a model block that exports functions, inherited rates are only expected in CodeInfo for DataStores. Note:  Before R2015a, CodeInterface  was called CodeUnderTest .
Workaround: Instead of using a Data Store Memory block, use a Simulink.Signal object to specify a global data store. An additional advantage with this approach is that the global data store is accessible across model reference boundaries.

Summary: airy(2,vpa(x)) sometimes returns results with insufficient accuracy.
Description: airy(2,vpa(x)) sometimes returns results that lack the required accuracy. For example, set the number of decimal digits to 32. Then compute the Airy function for the following double-precision value x  and for the variable-precision value vpa(x) . The results differ in the fourth digit after the decimal point. The double-precision result y  is correct. format longdigits(32)x = 15.5*exp(.37*pi*1i);y = airy(2, x)sy = airy(2, vpa(x))

Summary: Floating Scope blocks and Scope Viewers cannot place more than one signal on each display
Description: The assignment of signals to a Floating Scope and Viewer display (axis) has changed. Signals are placed one per display. If there are more signals than displays, the extra signals are plotted on the last display. In earlier versions, you were able to select signal assignments to create a custom layout. These custom assignments are lost when upgrading to R2015b. The signals are reassigned as described above. For example, a Floating Scope with 5 signals could display 3 signals on the first axis and 2 signals on the second axis. In R2015b, the signals are split with 1 signal in the first display and the remaining 4 signals in the second display.
Workaround: Add multiple Floating Scope blocks or Scope Viewers, and then attach the signals you want to overlay to each scope.Alternatively, you can use the attached script to redistribute signals to separate Floating Scope blocks or Scope Viewers. The order of signals is preserved. In the following example, a Floating Scope named 'Scope' with 5 signals is split into two Floating Scope blocks. Three signals are attached to the first scope block and two signals are attached to the second scope block.>>splitViewer(bdroot,'Scope',[3 2])

Summary: MATLAB crash caused by deleting a block with a broken library link that contains a test harness, then saving the model
Description: MATLAB crashes if you delete a block that meets both of the following criteria, and then save the model. The block has a test harness.  The block is an instance of a library link, and the link is broken.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Precision mismatch between simulation and code generation for n-D Lookup Table block when Intermediate results and Output Data Type is single and?Fraction Data Type is double
Description: n-D Lookup Table block may cause?a precision mismatch between simulation and code generation under these conditions: Intermediate results ?and?Output ?data type?are?single  Fraction   data type is double  Interpolation method  is? Linear   The precision difference is because?simulation and code generation use?different intermediate data types to interpolate. Formula for simulation:? y = (single)(f*(double)((double )T2- (double )T1)) + T1   Formula for code generation: y =?(single)(f*(double)((single )T2- (single )T1)) + T1   Where: y  ?is the output f  ? is the?fraction T  ?is?the?table value selected by the input and the breakpoints  Simulink prefers the code generation formula for blocks with these settings.
